name: Campus Living & Learning
description:
Campus Living & Learning

Living on campus at Baylor, you will experience a supportive Christian environment with people and services in place to help you achieve personal growth and academic success. You will have the opportunity to build significant relationships that will transform your life forever. You will experience what it means to be a part of something much bigger than yourself - a community.

Our Mission

Campus Living & Learning creates diverse residential environments that support learning and faith development through relationally driven communities.

Values

In Campus Living & Learning (CL&L), we hold deeply to the conviction that living and learning are inseparable pursuits and that faculty involvement is vital for the success of our residential communities. We believe that the best learning occurs through intentional efforts to create an atmosphere of academic pursuit in our residence halls. In accordance with this belief, CL&L's resolve is to support and create residential environments that are:

  • Conduits for Christian spiritual formation that foster the genuine pursuit of a relationship with God
  • Connections to intellectual growth and support for the academic mission of Baylor University
  • Places that encourage dialogue about student differences and foster relationships across socioeconomic, racial, ethnic, and religious lines
  • Communities of relationally authentic friendships where students pursue their vocation and calling through the exploration of strengths
Our Promise

Students will experience a community that enables them to author the life they have been called to live.
summary: Campus Living & Learning creates diverse residential environments that support learning and faith development through relationally driven communities.

name: Medical Ethics Discussion Society
description:
check us out at https://www.medsatbaylor.org!

MEDS is a pre-health organization dedicated to growing in our knowledge of medical ethics and building community among pre-health students at Baylor University. 

"Excellence through Medical Ethics" is our motto.  We're open to students from any major or designation, and we're passionate about the four pillars of medical ethics: justice, autonomy, beneficence, and non-maleficence.  

Discussion Based - We learn about medical ethics through weekly discussion and speaker meetings, focusing on case studies and contemporary issues in healthcare.

Growth Focused - We encourage members to participate in mentoring and workshop events to help grow their skills as future healthcare professionals.

Service Oriented - We have the opportunity to volunteer in the Waco community at locations like Mission Waco, Mosaic, Caritas, and the Regent Care Center.

Family Centered - We cultivate community as an organization through fun social events and regular study hours.
summary: MEDS is a pre-health organization dedicated to growing in our knowledge of medical ethics and building community among pre-health students at Baylor University. We host regular discussion and speaker meetings, and we volunteer in the Waco community.

Also, our annual Pre-Health Symposium, an event open to the Baylor Pre-Health community, promotes networking with over 40 different professional schools from Texas and the greater region. name: Student Court
description:
Student Court is the judicial branch of Student Government. It is composed of seven justices and two court clerks. Student Court interprets the official documents of Student Government and settles disputes among students and organizations. It also hears appeals concerning Baylor traffic tickets issued on campus. The Court works closely with the administration of the university - Student Life, Parking Services, and Judicial Affairs, as well as with the other branches of Student Government. For more information about the Court's jurisdiction see Article IV. Section V. of the Baylor Student Body Constitution.

All justices are appointed in accordance with the Student Body Constitution. This entails appointment by the Student Body President and then confirmation by a majority vote of the Student Senate. Each justice serves a term of two (undergraduate) academic years or until his or her graduation from Baylor University.

Individuals interested in serving on the Student Court as either a justice or a clerk should fill out the following application and submit it to the Student Body President. Candidates will be interviewed by the Student Body President and/or their staff and if selected will be nominated and required to be confirmed by the Student Senate.
summary: Student Court is the judicial branch of Student Government. It is composed of seven justices and two court clerks. Student Court interprets the official documents of Student Government and settles disputes among students and organizations.

name: Mu Phi Epsilon
description:
The Mu Phi Epsilon Phi Xi Chapter at Baylor University is over 65 years old.  Mu Phi Epsilon was originally founded as a music sorority in 1903 at the Metropolitan College of Music in, but later changed to a co-ed professional music fraternity in 1977. Mu Phi Epsilon has over 75,000 members in about 160 collegiate chapters across the United States and abroad.  The Phi Xi chapter at Baylor University is a nationally recognized chapter for our dedication to service to the Waco, Texas community and to the Baylor School of Music.

WE are a co-educational professional music fraternity whose mission is:
 
  • To advance music in the community, nation, and throughout the world;
  • To promote musicianship, scholarship, therapy, and education, with emphasis on service through music; and
  • To enhance loyalty to the Alma Mater and develop true bonds of friendship.
summary: The Mu Phi Epsilon Phi Xi Chapter at Baylor University is over 65 years old. We are a nationally recognized chapter for our dedication to service to the Waco community and to the Baylor University School of Music.

name: Alpha Tau Omega (Fraternity)
description:
Our national creed states:

To bind men together in a brotherhood based upon eternal and immutable principles, with a bond as strong as right itself and as lasting as humanity; to know no North, no South, no East, no West, but to know man as man, to teach that true men the world over should stand together and contend for supremacy of good over evil; to teach, not politics, but morals; to foster, not partisanship, but the recognition of true merit wherever found; to have no narrower limits within which to work together for the elevation of man than the outlines of the world: these were the thoughts and hopes uppermost in the minds of the founders of the Alpha Tau Omega Fraternity.

-Otis Allan Glazebrook 1880

 

Our local creed states:

I am a Baylor ATO

As a Baylor ATO, much is expected of me.

As a Baylor ATO, I am expected to be my best self at all times.

As a Baylor ATO, I hold my brothers accountable.

As a Baylor ATO, my brothers hold me accountable. 

As a Baylor ATO, I pledge to be a gentleman worthy of trust and respect. 

As a Baylor ATO, I will always try to be a worthy example of all men on campus.

As a Baylor ATO, I will strive every day to deserve to wear the letters. 

I am a Baylor ATO

 

As demonstrated by our national and local creeds, we are a group of brothers focused on holding each other accountable and promoting mutual growth. This is evident through our annually competitive Homecoming floats and 18 consecutive Pigskin acts. Baylor University recognized us as Chapter of the Year for all campus fraternities for the 2023-2024 year. Among other accomplishments, we were the all-university intramural champions, winning championships in football, soccer, and various other sports.

We also raised over $40,000 for our philanthropy, CoHope, through events such as the Chili Cook-Off and Bed Races, which we host in partnership with Chi O, as well as Race One and Viking Week. Additionally, we raised $30,000 for breast cancer research through our third annual Cuts-4-Cancer event in November 2024, where over 45 members shaved their heads.

Lastly, we hosted numerous social and brotherhood events, ranging from attending Dallas Stars and Austin FC games to our formal at Hotel 1928.

If you are interested in our recruitment process or would like to know more, do not hesitate to reach out to Levi Rothwell, our Fall Recruitment Chair. His number is (913) 909-4510‬.
summary: To be a brotherhood that satisfies the need for Godly young men to surround, edify, and support one another in an effort to live according to Christian morals and principles.

We have local optometrists and optometry school representatives speak to us throughout the year and we attend conferences to learn more about the field. We hope you will join our tight-knit community in the fall!

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description: The purpose and aims of College Chapters of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People shall be to improve the political, educational, social, and economic status of African Americans and other racial and ethnic minorities; to eliminate acial prejudice; to keep the public aware of the adverse effects of discrimination; to take lawful action to secure the elimination of racial discrimination; to seek legislation and policies at the local level,or at other levels if requested by the State/State-Area Conference or National Office,which advance the programs and policies of the Association; and to oppose legislation and policies which are adverse to the programs and policies of the Association consistent with the efforts of the national organization and in conformity with the Articles of Incorporation of the Association, its Constitution and Bylaws, and as directed by the Board of Directors.In addition, its objectives shall be to inform students of the problems affecting AfricanAmericans and other racial and ethnic minorities; to advance the economic, education, social,and political status of AfricanAmericans and other racial and ethnic minorities and their harmonious cooperation with other peoples; to stimulate an appreciation of the African Diaspora and other people of color’s contribution to civilization; and to develop an intelligent, militant, effective leadership. These objectives shall be pursued in accordance with the policies of the Association within the framework of university regulations.
summary: To build coalitions among people of various cultural and racial backgrounds and promote unity through Baylor University and the surrounding community.

name: Kappa Chi Alpha (Sorority)
description:
The purpose of Kappa Chi Alpha shall be for women to grow together in their walk with Christ and to share Him with others. Activities include prayer, Bible study, accountability, fellowship, and outreach.

 

 

KXA is a non-panhellenic Christian service sorority on the Baylor campus. We strive to live up to the meaning of Kappa Chi Alpha, which is "Keeping Christ First". 

 

 

To learn more about how to meet with membership and how to apply to our sorority, please visit https://www.kappachialpha.org/recruitment or email kxarecruitment@gmail.com
summary: The purpose of Kappa Chi Alpha shall be for women to grow together in their walk with Christ and to share Him with others. Activities include prayer, Bible study, accountability, fellowship, and outreach.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma (Sorority)
description: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks bonds of friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self-growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to positive ethical principles for every member throughout her life. Kappa Kappa Gamma was founded in 1870 at Monmouth College.
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks for every member throughout her life bonds of friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self-growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to posit...

name: Amateur Radio Club
description: To provide educational opportunities for students concerning the scope of amateur radio and radio license acquisition and provide opportunities for public service during emergencies and local charitable activities.
summary: To provide educational opportunities for students concerning the scope of amateur radio and radio license acquisition and provide opportunities for public service during emergencies and local charitable activities.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
description:
Alpha Phi Omega is a national co-educational service fraternity dedicated to fostering leadership, friendship, and service among its members. Our chapter engages in a wide range of service projects aimed at supporting the Baylor community, the Waco community, and those in need across the nation.

We believe in creating a well-rounded collegiate experience by also emphasizing fellowship through events such as formals, take-a-dates, leadership retreats, and tailgates. 

We invite all Baylor students, from freshman to senior and from any major, to learn more about our organization during our Recruitment Week. By becoming a part of Alpha Phi Omega, you will have the opportunity to develop your leadership skills, build lifelong friendships, and make a meaningful impact through service.
summary: Alpha Phi Omega is a national co-educational service fraternity dedicated to fostering leadership, friendship, and service among its members. Our chapter engages in a wide range of service projects aimed at supporting Baylor, Waco, and all those in need.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: Welcome! The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at Baylor is a non-exclusive organization whose purpose is to spread the Vietnamese culture as well as create a more diverse community on campus. We hope to raise awareness on the Vietnamese heritage and unify the Asian American community through our events such as Baylor Mid-Autumn Moon Festival, Baylor VSA Formal, and more. Through VSA, you will meet a group of students committed to fostering our three core values of culture, family, and community. We also compete in sports, play games, have food socials, and network with other VSAs or Asian organizations. Come join us!
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) at Baylor is a non-exclusive organization whose purpose is to celebrate Vietnamese culture as well as bring awareness to the Vietnamese and Vietnamese-American experience here on Baylor's campus.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description: The purpose of Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) will be to glorify God by sharing the message of Jesus Christ with the Baylor community in word and deed, and to equip students to serve the Church, on the campus, and in the community and world, and to live all of life through a worldview that is biblically and historically Reformed and Covenantal in understanding as summarized in the Westminster Confession of Faith and Catechisms.
summary: The purpose of Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) will be to glorify God by sharing the message of Jesus Christ with the Baylor community in word and deed, and to equip students to serve the Church, on the campus, and in the community and world, and ...

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description: Baylor’s National Society of Collegiate Scholars Chapter is unique because we provide opportunities to make a difference. March to College Day - an activity that truly sets us apart from other organizations - is our chance to make a positive difference in the lives of high school students aspiring to come to college. We give them a tour of our campus, invite them into our classrooms, and have lunch where we engage in extensive conversations about college life. Furthermore, we have many social, community service, and professional development events throughout the year – Movie Nights, Active Member Parties, and Steppin’ Out to name a few – that truly makes the NSCS experience a good one! We look forward to starting the year off with you all!
summary: The Baylor Chapter of NSCS is a unique home for students of all majors. We focus on scholarship, leadership, and service by providing outreach opportunities for those involved. Look for an invitation from the National Organization before joining!

name: Red Cross at Baylor University
description: To advocate for and educate the Baylor community about disasters and disaster relief and to assist the American Red Cross and its mission to provide aid, through the efforts of volunteers and donors, to those affected by natural and man-made tragedies
summary: To advocate for and educate the Baylor community about disasters and disaster relief and to assist the American Red Cross and its mission to provide aid, through the efforts of volunteers and donors, to those affected by natural and man-made tragedies.

name: Future Nurses Association at Baylor
description:
The Future Nurses Association at Baylor is dedicated to cultivating community within the nursing cohorts. We provide students with social, spiritual, and academic support, as well as volunteering opportunities, to better prepare them for nursing school.

 

With study groups, social events, and ways to get involved around the Waco community, FNA is a safe haven for nursing students, providing not only academic, but emotional support. At the end of each meeting, there is an optional devotional and prayer for those who may need spiritual guidance. 

 

As prospective nurses, we have a heart to serve others, and we don't have to wait until nursing school!

Join us at soup kitchens, Compassion Waco, donation drives, and more to learn how to love the community that serves us daily!
summary: The Future Nurses Association at Baylor is dedicated to cultivating community within the nursing cohorts. FNA provides students with social, spiritual, and academic support, as well as volunteering opportunities, to prepare them for nursing school.

name: Kappa Omega Tau (Fraternity)
description: The purpose of Kappa Omega Tau is to promote service throughout the Baylor community and Waco as a whole while building lasting relationship with peers.
summary: The purpose of Kappa Omega Tau is to promote service throughout the Baylor community and Waco as a whole while building lasting relationship with peers.

name: Oso Esports
description: The purpose of OSO Esports is to promote & develop the emergence of Esports in Baylor leading to Baylor unity in video gaming and entertainment. We also want to advocate Esports legitimacy on a campus already invested in traditional sports and create an environment that welcomes students of all backgrounds. Join at https://discord.gg/rgkhA7AS7v for more information.
summary: The purpose of Oso Esports is to promote & develop the emergence of esports in Baylor leading to Baylor unity in video gaming and entertainment.

name: Silver Wings
description: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.
summary: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

name: Chess Society at Baylor
description: To study, play, and promote the game of chess. Weekly meetings on Fridays- all are welcome!
summary: To study, play, and promote the game of chess.

name: Anglican Student Ministries
description:
ASM exists to glorify God as an authentically Anglican, classically Christian community within Baylor University. We are associated with the Anglican Church in North America and sponsored by Christ Church Waco.

Join us for evensong, Chick-Fil-A, and fellowship on Wednesdays at 6:30 p.m. in Robbins Chapel!
summary: To glorify God as authentically Anglican, classically Christian community within Baylor University. We are associated with the Anglican Church of North America and the Episcopal Diocese of Fort Worth.

name: Hispanic Student Association
description: The Hispanic Student Association at Baylor University was founded in 1987. The organization maintains an integral role on campus as it promotes diversity, inclusivity, and collaboration, and educates the community about Hispanic culture, heritage, and traditions. HSA's mission statement: "To foster an enriching experience of the Hispanic culture, heritage, and traditions in the Baylor and Waco communities while empowering and supporting students with their personal development, and advocating for a diverse and inclusive environment" HSA's Four Core Values: Diversity, Harmony, Scholarship, and Development
summary: The Hispanic Student Association at Baylor University was founded in 1987. The organization maintains an integral role on campus as it promotes diversity, inclusivity, and collaboration. We also strive to educate about Hispanic culture and heritage.

name: Baylor Men's Volleyball Club
description: We are a nationally competitive club that competes in the Southern Intercollegiate Volleyball Association (SIVA) and the National Collegiate Volleyball Federation (NCVF). We travel across the southern U.S. and recently finished 18th at the NCVF National Championships. We welcome students who share a passion for high-level volleyball and team camaraderie. We play under U.S. Volleyball Association rules.
summary: We are a competitive club that competes in the Southern Independent Volleyball Association (SIVA) and the National Collegiate Volleyball Federation (NCVF). Students who share our passion for the sport are invited to join.

name: Filipino Student Association
description: The Filipino Student Association was founded at Baylor University in 1997. Our mission is to provide inviting, open spaces to build relationships, embrace diversity, and foster personal growth while sharing Filipino culture. FSA's three pillars are "Family, Team, and Fun".
summary: The Filipino Student Association was founded at Baylor University in 1997. Our mission is to provide inviting, open spaces to build relationships, embrace diversity, and foster personal growth while sharing Filipino culture.

name: Baylor Running Club
description: We are the Baylor Running Club! We plan to meet a few days out of the week for group runs and hope to enter some local competitions and races! Whether you're a seasoned runner or a new runner, we welcome runners of all fitness levels! Please join our GroupMe to get notifications on meetings!
summary: The purpose of the Baylor Running Club is to provide Baylor students the opportunity to meet and run with other students on a regular basis in order to improve their overall health and get involved in the running community.

name: Urban Dance Society
description:
Welcome to UDS!! Like to dance? For 16 years, Urban Dance Society, Baylor's only hip-hop dance student organization, would love to have you. We offer a fun and friendly atmosphere and the opportunity to learn all styles within the urban dance umbrella such as hip-hop, jazz-funk, cultural dance, popping, locking, breaking, and more. All levels of experience are welcome. We also have a performance team for those people who want more than the weekly meetings and are interested in performing at different campus and Waco events.

Questions? Contact Isla Ritchie!
summary: Like to dance? Urban Dance Society offers a fun and friendly atmosphere and the opportunity to learn all styles within the hip-hop dance umbrella such as hip-hop, jazz-funk, cultural dance, popping, locking, breaking, and more.

name: Kappa Phi Gamma (Sorority)
description: Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. was established on November 8th, 1998 as the Nation’s first South Asian (not exclusively) Interest-Based Sorority. Sisterhood ties Kappa Phi Gamma together and unites any and all motivated young women who are willing to advance womanhood and make a difference at her University and community. Kappa Phi Gamma aims to instill integrity of character and trust into personality and encourage bonding between women to strengthen sisterhood. Importantly, the sisters participate in service and social events to raise money towards our philanthropy, Cancer Awareness. As a sorority who prioritizes educational successes and accomplishments, the sisterhood shall continue to develop leadership skills and high academic standards that follow into the professional world. Together, our sisters promote pride of culture, womanhood, and self to better one another and society around us.
summary: Kappa Phi Gamma Sorority, Inc. was established on November 8th, 1998 as the Nation’s first South Asian (not exclusively) Interest-Based Sorority. Sisterhood unites any and all motivated young women who are willing to advance womanhood and make an impact.

name: Indian Subcontinent Student Association
description: The purpose of this organization is to provide unity among students of the Indian Subcontinent through service and cultural sharing, in the effort to serve the Baylor community. ISSA members participate in various service, social, cultural, and intramural events throughout the year. ISSA's philanthropy is Developments in Literacy who educate underprivileged kids in Pakistan.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide unity among students of the Indian Subcontinent through service and cultural sharing, in the effort to serve the Baylor community.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is one of the largest and most respected professional business fraternities in the world, with over 300,000 members. Rooted in four core pillars: Professionalism, Community Service, Scholarship, and Brotherhood, we equip our members with the tools to thrive in business and beyond.

Our extensive alumni network spans industries like banking, tech, sales, and consulting (and many more) providing unmatched opportunities to build lifelong connections and jumpstart your career.

Link to our interest form: https://forms.gle/hWG9maGQFvdDd9kYA
summary: An international business fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities. Co-educational and open to all business majors. Activities include professional programs, service projects, and social activities.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: Orthodox Christian Fellowship (OCF) seeks to transform the lives of Baylor students by guiding them along the path to Jesus Christ through His Church, cultivating a campus community of worship, witness, service, fellowship and education. We welcome all students, both those already Orthodox and those interested in learning about Orthodox Christianity. Staff, and faculty are also welcome to attend. OCF is the official campus ministry organization of the Assembly of Canonical Bishops of the United States of America.
summary: OCF is a fellowship for all students - already Orthodox or just curious - who are interested in deepening their relationship with Christ and learning more about Orthodox Christianity. Come and see.

name: Steppin' Out Planning Committee
description: A student committee that works behind the scenes to plan the Steppin Out Day of Service. The students who plan Steppin' Out come from various majors and work hard to meaningfully represent Baylor to the Waco community.
summary: A student committee that works behind the scenes to plan the Steppin' Out Day of Service. The students who plan Steppin' Out come from various majors and work hard to meaningfully represent Baylor to the Waco community.

name: Nu Rho Psi
description: To encourage and recognize excellence in scholarship, especially in neuroscience, to promote leadership and encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty and professionals.
summary: To encourage and recognize excellence in scholarship, especially in neuroscience, to promote leadership and encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty and professionals.

name: National Student Speech-Language-Hearing Association
description: Organization for students interested in communication sciences and disorders. This includes speech pathology, audiology, deaf education, and ASL. The NSSLHA chapter at Baylor University represents an inclusive and diverse network of students with similar interests. Members have the opportunity to hear from professionals working in the field and to participate in a variety of social, service, and advocacy events.
summary: Organization for students interested in communication sciences and the speech pathology, audiology profession. Membership includes social, service, and advocacy events.

name: Sociology of Undergraduate Lives (SOUL)
description: Our purpose is to provide a new method of applying Sociological concepts outside of the classroom. We aim to serve Sociology students looking for a community they can deeply invest in while diving deeper into their area of study. We not only intend to learn from each other, but also share our academic interests in the form of an online journal. This journal will be open to all students, faculty, and staff, and will strive to exemplify how Sociology directly relates to all lives, as well as how we as an organization contribute to Sociological research.
summary: Our purpose is to provide a new method of applying Sociological concepts outside of the classroom. We aim to serve Sociology students looking for a community they can deeply invest in while diving deeper into their area of study.

name: College Democrats of Baylor
description: The College Democrats of Baylor are a politically active and engaged group on campus that promotes progressive ideals and goals through activism, mutual aid, and community engagement. We execute this mission by creating a space for progressive students on campus to meet and discuss with each other, holding voter registration drives, and fostering civic engagement on campus and within the greater Waco community. In doing so, we seek to promote a culture centered on genuine empathy, compassion and love for one’s neighbor throughout Baylor and locally in our city. TLDR, we’re unapologetic progressives who seek to break the conservative status quo of our institutions however we can.
summary: We provide Baylor University students a means to be politically active and engaged with Democratic and left-wing ideals and goals through activism and community engagement. In doing so we unapologetically promote progressive ideals on campus and in Waco.

name: Asian Ministry InterVarsity
description:
Welcome to AMIV! We are an on-campus ministry that desires to see Asian Americans transformed as we explore what it means to follow Jesus together. We gather in small groups and large groups and spend time together through various activities that foster community and fellowship. Because our heritage is built on love for and celebration of food, we also enjoy gathering over meals, cooking, and more! Although we are an ethnic-specific ministry, we are not exclusive to Asians or Christians.

Follow us @bayloramiv on instagram to stay up to date with our events!
summary: Welcome to AMIV! We are an on-campus ministry that desires to see Asian Americans transformed, as we explore what it means to follow Jesus together. We gather in small groups and large groups every week to foster community and fellowship.

name: Arnold Air Society
description: National honorary service organization for Air Force ROTC cadets serving the AFROTC, Baylor, and Waco community. Projects include food drives, clean ups, and various volunteering and support projects. Membership open only to AFROTC cadets.
summary: National honorary service organization for Air Force ROTC cadets serving the AFROTC, Baylor, and Waco community. Projects include aiding disadvantaged citizens, POW/MIA Awareness, and support projects for Baylor AFROTC.

name: Baylor Crew
description: Baylor Crew is the collegiate rowing team of Baylor University. Although not affiliated with the NCAA, our co-ed club competes in regattas against colleges across the country. No experience? No problem. In fact, the majority of our members never rowed before joining Baylor Crew. Our coaches are qualified and more than happy to teach you not only the basics, but also how to excel alongside stiff competition. Rowing requires long hours of practice and training while maintaining academic excellence. Baylor Crew embodies the time-honored tradition of the collegiate athlete as both sportsman and scholar. We pride ourselves in mastering this challenging sport that demands dedication, focus, and teamwork. It’s not all hard labor and early hours, however. Baylor Crew strives to create a fun, uplifting community both at practice and outside of workouts. Social events are offered throughout the semester, including pre-regatta pasta parties, Crew Semi-Formal, and Crew-B-Q. The relationships formed through Crew reach beyond the sport. The teammates you lose, win, and sweat with often become some of your closest friends at Baylor
summary: Baylor Crew is the collegiate rowing team of Baylor University. Our team is made up of rowers of varying experience and ages. We focus on teaching the sport, creating a competitive atmosphere, and providing a community for all, here at Baylor University.

name: Vertical Ministries
description: Vertical Ministries was created as a non-denominational, student led ministry that exists to ignite a passion in college students for Jesus Christ, His church, and His mission around the world.
summary: Vertical Ministries was created as a non-denominational, student led ministry that exists to ignite a passion in college students for Jesus Christ, His church, and His mission around the world.

name: Unite Intervarsity Christian Fellowship
description: We are a welcoming, collaborative community that pursues God through Bible study, worship, and service in order to show God's love to campus. UNITE Intervarsity is a multi-ethnic group with a special focus on grad student ministry.
summary: We are a welcoming, collaborative community that pursues God through Bible study, worship, and service in order to show God's love to campus. UNITE Intervarsity is a multi-ethnic group with a special focus on grad student ministry

name: Japanese Student Association
description: Yōkoso! Be a part of BU’s Japanese Student Association, a social gathering for all students interested in engaging with the Japanese culture. Our association strives to bring together the students at BU while raising awareness and appreciation of the Japanese culture and unifying the Asian American community, thus helping to build a more diverse community through various cultural events and activities such as our annual harumatsuri and lots of delicious food!  All students are welcome – whether you are an exchange or international student or simply interested in the Japanese culture, be a part of this exciting adventure.  Dozo irasshai!
summary: The Baylor Japanese Student Association focuses on promoting and learning more about Japanese culture, language, and more!

name: Roots Natural Hair Club
description: The purpose of Roots is to be an outlet for Black and African American students to learn about their natural hair and overall health. Further, this organization serves as a judgement free zone to promote unity, knowledge, and growth.
summary: The purpose of Roots shall be to foster community among men and women with natural hair, educate all students about hair science as it pertains to natural hair, and to celebrate the role of hair in the African American culture.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
description: Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) is the honor society within IEEE for high-achieving juniors, seniors, and all graduate students in Electrical & Computer Engineering. The goal of the Kappa Tau chapter of HKN is to promote leadership, scholarship, and professional development among these students.
summary: Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) is the honor society within IEEE for high-achieving juniors, seniors, and all graduate students in ECE. The goal of the Kappa Tau chapter of HKN is to promote leadership, scholarship, and professional development.

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon (Fraternity)
description: Lambda Phi Epsilon is the world's largest and ONLY international Asian-interest, but not Asian-exclusive, Fraternity founded on our core values of Love, Wisdom, Cultural Heritage, Courageous Leadership, and Authenticity. The purpose of Lambda Phi Epsilon is to promote academic achievement and provide philanthropy for worthy causes while promoting interaction among people of different ethnic heritages.
summary: The purpose of Lambda Phi Epsilon is to promote academic achievement and provide philanthropy for worthy causes while promoting interaction among people of different ethnic heritages.

name: Kappa Sigma (Fraternity)
description: Kappa Sigma is a social fraternity with the purpose of promoting fellowship and brotherhood among its members. Kappa Sigma prides itself on the diversity of its members who, united under the bond of brotherhood and friendship, strive for excellence in all that they do. Members are expected to encourage the academic proficiency, spiritual development, and personal growth of their respective brothers. The Lambda Tau chapter of Kappa Sigma also seeks to improve the greater Baylor community and beyond with acts of service and philanthropic missions, including the Military Heroes Campaign and the annual "Stars and Strides" event.
summary: Kappa Sigma is a social fraternity with the purpose of promoting fellowship and a sense of brotherhood among its respective members...

name: Korean Student Association
description: The Korean Student Association is a social organization for students of all races who have an interest in the Korean culture. It is also an outlet for the Korean population at Baylor to better situate themselves in a comfortable environment and provide a chance to make new long-lasting friendships. This organization enables students to express themselves in the language and culture that they love and continue to grow within it!
summary: The Korean Student Association is a social organization for students of all races who have an interest in the Korean culture. This organization enables students to express themselves in the language and culture that they love and continue to grow within!

name: The Campus Kitchen at Baylor University
description:
Campus Kitchen is a volunteer-driven organization dedicated to nourishing the Baylor and Waco communities through cooking nutritious meals at Penland Dining Hall and tending the Baylor Community Garden. Our mission is to serve others by providing fresh meals and fostering sustainable, community-centered food practices.

The Campus Kitchen at Baylor University is an affiliate of the national organization, Campus Kitchens Project (CKP), which is a national leader in community service for students and the future of hunger relief.
summary: To use service as a tool to (1) strengthen bodies by using existing resources to meet hunger and nutritional needs, (2) empower minds by providing leadership and service opportunities to students and community, (3) build communities by fostering a new...

name: Sailing Club
description: A member of the Southeastern Intercollegiate Sailing Association, this organization promotes competitive collegiate sailing on the Baylor campus. We welcome members with various levels of experience, including those who want to learn. We sail on FJ's, a two-crew sailboat, on Lake Waco weekly, weather permitting. Our season is year-round, however, we are limited by the temperature of the air and the water during the winter months.
summary: A member of the Southeastern Intercollegiate Sailing Association, this organization promotes competitive collegiate sailing on the Baylor campus. We welcome members with various levels of experience, including those who want to learn.

name: Taal at Baylor University
description: A bollywood-fushion competitive dance team with aspirations of spreading awareness of the diversity and beauty of South-Asian culture through means of dance.
summary: To promote diversity and spread South-Asian culture to Baylor University's campus and the Waco community through the expression of dance forms such as Bollywood, Bhangra, Hip-hop, and Indian Classical.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description: It is the mission of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. to enhance the quality of life for women and their families in the U.S. and globally through community service, civil, and social action. Our goal is to achieve greater progress in the areas of education, health awareness, and leadership development. Our members, affiliates, staff, and community partners work to create and support initiatives that align with our vision.
summary: Our mission is to enhance the quality of life for women and their families in the U.S. and globally through community service, civil, and social action. Our goal is to achieve greater progress in education, health awareness, and leadership development.

name: Sigma Chi (Fraternity)
description: The fundamental purpose of Sigma Chi is the cultivation, maintenance and accomplishment of the ideals of friendship, justice, and learning within our membership. We are committed to the development of values-based leaders committed to the betterment of character, campus, and community.
summary: Sigma Chi is a multi-national fraternity with 244 chapters and 300,000 members.

name: Baylor Buddies
description: Baylor Buddies is a mentoring organization that works to foster mentoring relationships between elementary and middle school-aged children at risk of dropping out of school in the Waco community. Mentors are required to meet with the student they mentor on their school campus for one hour per week until the end of the school year. Members mentor their Buddy whenever they're available during their school day from 8 AM to 3 PM! Mentors typically play board games, play sports, or just talk with their mentee during mentoring sessions. In addition to mentoring, members attend meetings and participate in events with their mentee hosted by Baylor Buddies. We host mentor appreciation events 1-2 times a semester for our awesome mentors and we also host 1-2 "All Organization Events" throughout the semester! The "All Organization Events" can be anything from a trip to Top Golf to a fun movie night at Baylor. We hope that the "All Organization Events" give members the opportunity to form a special bond with their Buddy and spend some quality time with their buddy outside of school! We have no general meetings, and attending our events is not required. It's really simple and easy to join! visit https://missions.web.baylor.edu/local-service/serve-group for more details and to get started on your application!
summary: Departmental organization facilitating mentoring relationships between Baylor students and elementary and middle school-aged children at risk of dropping out of school in the Waco community.

name: BRH Choir
description: The BRH Choir is one of the oldest student-led organizations at Baylor University. Founded by Dick Baker in 1948, BRH has ministered to Baylor through regular worship services, to churches across Texas through weekend travel concerts, and to the global Church through its annual international mission trip. The choir is made up of approximately fifty auditioned individuals from all areas of study that come together to worship the Lord through music and fellowship.
summary: The BRH Choir is a student-led choir that has been on-campus since 1948. BRH ministers to the Church as a whole through regular worship services, weekend travel concerts, and an annual international mission trip.

name: Young Life College at Baylor
description: Young Life College seeks to form a community of college students that are fun, genuine, and consistent so that all students feel as though they belong to something that is bigger than themselves! Our main outreach goal is to provide a platform for all students to hear the gospel of Jesus Christ. In ministry, we also hope to equip college-aged leaders to go into the Waco community to spread the Gospel and the love of God with middle schoolers, high schoolers, college students, and friends with disabilities.
summary: Provide an environment that allows students to grow in community with others and hear the gospel of Jesus Christ

name: Sing Alliance
description: Sing Alliance is a student-led organization at Baylor University that gives ALL students the opportunity to participate in All-University Sing, a competition of 7-minute, Broadway-style productions that has been a Baylor tradition since 1953. We have three goals: to have fun, make new friends, and perform to the best of our abilities
summary: Sing Alliance is a student-led organization at Baylor University that gives ALL students the opportunity to participate in All-University Sing, a competition of 7-minute, Broadway-style productions that has been a Baylor tradition since 1953. We have t...

name: Engineers with a Mission
description: Our purpose is to motivate and train engineering students by creating opportunities for them to discover and develop their vocations as ministers of reconciliation in a world of poverty, injustice, and suffering. Through word, deed, and design, we share the gospel of Christ in a way that is reciprocal and relationship-focused so that we might transform others as we, ourselves, are transformed.
summary: Engineers with a Mission is a group of students with the desire to shape the mindset of engineers to become ministers of reconciliation in this world through sharing the Gospel of Christ in word and deed.

name: Habitat for Humanity at Baylor University
description:
We are a non-profit organization that works to eliminate sub-standard housing & homelessness worldwide. Seeking to put God’s love into action, Habitat for Humanity brings people together to build homes, communities, and hope. No home remodeling / construction experience required!

 

Check out a local H4H homeowner's story here.
summary: We are a non-profit organization that works to eliminate sub-standard housing & homelessness worldwide.

name: Catholic Student Association
description:
We strive to promote Catholicism through events intended to unite the Catholic community at Baylor University. CSA serves as the bridge between Baylor and St. Peter's Catholic Student Center! We host many fellowship events to further the sense of community and friendship among Catholic Baylor students. We also promote the moral, intellectual, social, and spiritual growth of our community through speakers and events geared toward faith, service, and fellowship.

CSA is open to all Baylor students, undergrads and graduate students. Members attend monthly General Meetings and help officers plan and promote events.

Have questions or want to join? Reach out to csa@baylorcatholic.org!
summary: Catholic Student Association (CSA) serves Catholic students at Baylor University by furthering the moral, intellectual, social, and spiritual lives of members through events geared toward faith and friendship.

name: Baylor Club Quadball
description: The Quadball team meets two to three times a week to practice our sport and grow in community with one another. Roughly once a month, we attend tournaments to compete against other universities and create lasting bonds between schools through healthy competition. We also host regular service and social events to have fun together as a team and give back to our Waco community. Our focus is to build a community and a healthy team culture through the activity of playing Quadball.
summary: A competitive and social club sport focused on fostering relationships between Baylor students and competing against other universities.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council (Fraternity/Sorority)
description: The purpose of the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is to serve as the official coordinating agent of the constituent member NPHC fraternities and sororities in the furtherance of their program unity at Baylor and within the community.
summary: The purpose of the National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) is to serve as the official coordinating agent of the constituent member NPHC fraternities and sororities in the furtherance of their program unity at Baylor and within the community.

name: Beta Theta Pi (Fraternity)

name: Phi Beta Sigma (Fraternity)
description:
Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity was founded at Howard University in Washington, D.C., on January 9, 1914, by three young African-American male students. The Founders, Honorable A. Langston Taylor, Honorable Leonard F. Morse, and Honorable Charles I. Brown, wanted to organize a Greek letter fraternity that would truly exemplify the ideals of brotherhood, scholarship, and service.

 

The Founders deeply wished to create an organization that viewed itself as “a part of” the general community rather than “apart from” the general community. They believed that each potential member should be judged by his own merits, rather than his family background or affluence…without regard to race, nationality, skin tone, or texture of hair. They desired for their fraternity to exist as part of an even greater brotherhood that would be devoted to the “inclusive we” rather than the “exclusive we”.

 

From its inception, the Founders also conceived Phi Beta Sigma as a mechanism to deliver services to the general community. Rather than gaining skills to be utilized exclusively for themselves and their immediate families, they held a deep conviction that they should return their newly acquired skills to the communities from which they had come. This deep conviction was mirrored in the Fraternity’s motto, “Culture For Service and Service For Humanity”.

 

Today, Phi Beta Sigma has blossomed into an international organization of leaders. No longer a single entity, members of the Fraternity have been instrumental in the establishment of the Phi Beta Sigma National Foundation, the Phi Beta Sigma Federal Credit Union, and The Sigma Beta Club Foundation. Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, founded in 1920 with the assistance of Phi Beta Sigma, is the sister organization of the Fraternity.
summary: The purpose of Phi Beta Sigma is to reaffirm and maintain a strong commitment to brotherhood, scholarship, and service and to ensure fraternity programs are focused and committed to serving humanity.

name: Ratio Christi
description:
Ratio Christi (Latin for ‘The Reason of Christ’) is a global movement that equips university students and faculty to give theological, historical, philosophical, and scientific reasons for following Jesus Christ. Bringing together faith and reason to establish the intellectual voice of Christ in the University, Ratio Christi is planting student and faculty-led apologetics clubs at universities around the world. The Baylor chapter of Ratio Christi, previously known as “OSO Logos”, was founded in Waco, Texas, and is a university-approved student organization.

The Baylor chapter aims to encourage students to interact with their faith through tabling events (asking students in the community questions about why and what they believe), small group events (“Theologos”, which guides students in an in-depth study about specific apologetics topics from renowned writers), and speaker events (general meetings as well as panels and outside speakers).

 

Chapter Director:

Carl Schubert, Ratio Christi Representative

description: The Leadership Living Learning Center (LEAD LLC) is a four-year residential experience that challenges and equips students to live a more meaningful life, and to influence others to make a positive impact on their world. LEAD cultivates students' leadership ability by preparing them for and engaging them in hands on experiences and by providing continuous feedback and reflection on their leadership development. Reprising Baylor's Leadership Fellows program, LEAD is one of Baylor's flagship living learning communities. LEAD intentionally connects Baylor's living and learning initiatives to its core commitment to leadership and service.
summary: The Leadership Living Learning Center (LEAD LLC) is a four-year residential experience that challenges and equips students to live a more meaningful life, and to influence others to make a positive impact on their world. LEAD cultivates students' leade...

name: Association for Women in Mathematics
description: AWM's purpose is to encourage women to study, and have active careers in, the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal treatment and opportunities within the field. Our mission is to facilitate activities that support this purpose, both at the graduate and undergraduate levels. We organize events such as social gatherings, presentations, panel discussions, etc., and offer professional development. We emphasize that the chapter is open to all genders and to both graduate students and undergraduates.
summary: AWM's purpose is to encourage women to study, and have active careers in, the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal treatment and opportunities within the field. Our mission is to facilitate activities that support this purpose, both at the gradu...

name: Baylor Waterski Team
description: We are a Co-Ed Club Sport that is active in the South Central Region. We are open to anyone who wants to come out and enjoy the sport! Feel free to shoot us an e-mail, text or call anytime if you want more info or are interested!  Follow us on Instagram @bu_waterski
summary: We are a Co-Ed Club Sport that is open to anyone who wants to come out and enjoy the sport! Feel free to shoot us an e-mail, text or call anytime if you want more info or are interested! Follow us on Instagram @bu_waterski

name: Kendo Club at Baylor University
description:
A club dedicated to the practice of Kendo through discipline, honor, and fun!

Join our weekly practice every Thursdays from 7:30 pm to 8:30 pm at Marrs McLean Gym Gymnasium exclusively for Baylor students. Some members also practice on Tuesday from 7:00-8:30 pm and Saturday from 9:30 am to 12 pm at the Dorris Miller Community Center as Waco Kendo Dojo/Club. If you are interested in Kendo please reach out to one of the officers to learn about how to become a member of the club.
summary: A club dedicated to the practice of Kendo, the Art of the Japanese Sword, through discipline, honor, and fun! We welcome all levels of experience!

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
description: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is a non-partisan, non-profit national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch young people into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with a perspective of principled U.S. leadership.
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is a non-partisan, non-profit national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch young people into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with a perspective of principled U.S. leadership.

name: Global Surgery Student Alliance
description: GSSA is the national student-run global surgery working group located at medical schools and undergraduate institutions across the US. We believe that students of all disciplines are a valuable resource for the advancement of global surgery. We also believe that being unified in our efforts will help further our initiatives and can also facilitate more collaboration amongst overall global surgery efforts, both now and in the future. By exposing students to the field early on, we hope to educate, inspire and unite students through engagement and mentorship in global surgery.
summary: GSSA is an organization dedicated to educating, inspiring and uniting students through engagement and mentorship in global surgery

name: Bears' Association of Human Resources
description:
The Bears' Association of Human Resources (BAHR) is a student chapter of the Society for Human Resource Management, sponsored by the Dallas HR professional chapter. 

BAHR's mission is to create a supportive and empowering community that fosters growth in HR knowledge, facilitates networking with professionals, and builds lasting relationships among students and faculty, ultimately preparing students for successful and impactful careers in Human Resource Management.

BAHR typically meets around twice per month and it is open to all students. All events are designed to give you valuable information in your own professional development journey and to help you connect in meaningful ways with other students, faculty and industry partners.
summary: The Bears' Association of Human Resources (BAHR) is a student chapter of the Society for Human Resource Management, sponsored by Dallas HR. BAHR meets twice monthly, offering events that enhance HR knowledge, foster networking, and successful careers!

name: Baylor Men's Rugby Club
description: The Baylor Men's Rugby Club is a band of brothers that push each other to be the best version of ourselves. This includes faith, fitness, school, family, and life.
summary: The Baylor Men's Rugby Club is a band of brothers that push each other to be the best version of ourselves. This includes faith, fitness, school, family, and life.

name: Spikeball Club at Baylor
description: Come join the Spikeball club! We play on the Fountain Mall fields throughout the year and are excited to get new people spiking! Feel free to reach out to Micah Allin (club president micah_allin1@baylor.edu) if you're interested.
summary: This sport is very fast paced and is a lot of fun to play! The sport is growing right now and we are here to help that growth along with growing in community with our members.

If you would like to join or have any questions, email us at theclassicalballetsociety@gmail.com!

name: Brooks Residential College
description: Brooks Residential College is modelled on years of successful and traditional living learning programs such as those found at Oxford, Cambridge, Harvard and Yale. As the first and oldest residential college on Baylor’s campus, Brooks is an established, engaging and vibrant community with a trans-disciplinary focus, open to all academic majors and classifications.
summary: Brooks Residential College is modelled on years of successful and traditional living learning programs such as those found at Oxford, Cambridge, Harvard and Yale. As the first and oldest residential college on Baylor’s campus, Brooks is an established,...

name: Collins Residence Hall
description: Collins Hall is home to over 600 first-year female residents of all majors. Residents enjoy activities that help them build friendships, connect to campus life, and grow their faith and cultural competence. Collins includes a large first floor lounge with space for studying, socializing, and exercising. Collins Hall is located near Pat Neff Hall.
summary: Collins Hall is home to over 600 first-year female residents of all majors. Residents enjoy activities that help them build friendships, connect to campus life, and grow their faith and cultural competence. Collins includes a large first floor lounge w...

name: Face Time with God
description: The Face Time family is a community of students committed to authenticity and intimacy with God. We believe that it is within a personal relationship that God transforms us to pursue peace and unity through His Spirit and further transform our world. We gather on Thursdays at 7pm to study the Word, worship together and for fellowship with each other!
summary: At FaceTime with God, we keep it 100! We are a family committed to building authentic and intimate relationships with God for personal and societal transformation.

name: Christian Pre-Health Fellowship
description: Christian Pre-Health Fellowship (CPF) is a close-knit community of pre-health students who are seeking to understand how our faith underlies and transforms our calling to work in the healthcare field. Our mission is to connect Christian pre-health students, encourage them in their vocational journey, and demonstrate the value of their call to medical ministry at home and abroad. We hope to help our members experience joyful, authentic, Christ-centered community as they pursue careers that can be dominated by intense competition--something we accomplish through events like tailgates and game nights in addition to local and international service, corporate prayer and worship, and conversations with Christian healthcare professionals.
summary: Our mission is to connect Christian pre-health students, encourage them in their vocational journey, and honor their call to medical ministry at home and abroad. We have prayer, worship, speakers, service, and meetings to fellowship together.

name: Computing for Compassion
description:
Computing for Compassion is a service-oriented organization that has a strong emphasis on academic growth and professional development. Our mission is to serve compassion-based organizations through the appropriate application of technology. We help provide technological solutions and education to local schools, non-profits, and churches in the Waco, TX community. We hope to bring together students with an interest in technology in order to provide a place to be able to give back to Baylor and the Waco community. All majors are welcome to join.

Join our GroupMe!

https://groupme.com/join_group/110032832/CQCD2WpJ
summary: Computing for Compassion is a collection of like-minded Baylor students from all backgrounds who are passionate about empowering and enriching the greater Waco community through technology.

name: Union Board
description:
The Union Board exists to create and sustain programs, within the Bill Daniel Student Center, that are both engaging and enriching in order to foster community and relationships among one another. We aspire to create a welcoming environment that allows students to feel at home and express their individuality. This shall be accomplished by creating events that respect and applaud differences in the student body.

Union Board Programs:

Sundown Sessions is dedicated to providing dynamic and entertaining events, throughout the year, that enhance the overall Baylor experience. Hosted in the Bill Daniel Student Center on Friday and Saturday evenings (8pm-11pm), Sundown Sessions offers a variety of programs such as movie nights, live musical performances, and interactive activities that provide pathways for authentic connection, inclusive community, and a place to call home.

Ubreak, a biweekly free pop-up brunch bar hosted in front of the UB room in the SUB, strives to remind students the importance of taking a break in the midst of their busy schedules to be in community with each other.

The Big Event is co-hosted with the department of New Student Programs. The Big Event is exactly what it sounds like: a massive party to kick off the school year! The event takes over the SUB, morphing nearly every space into a different experience. From DJs to laser tag, students enjoy an evening filled with activities.

Christmas on 5th is Baylor’s official celebration of the advent season. Though headed by BAC, CO5 is an enterprise-wide collaboration that enlists the support of all three programming boards, multiple departments, and 5 campus venues.
summary: The Union Board exists to create and sustain programs, within the Bill Daniel Student Center, that are both engaging and enriching in order to foster community and relationships among one another.

name: Baylor Club Climbing
description: Baylor Club Climbing offers a welcoming community to those who enjoy climbing and are looking to compete! The team is open to all skill levels whether you are a total beginner or you're sending the hardest routes! We offer optional climbing competitions and events each semester. Practice occurs twice a week from 8-10pm at the Baylor ROCK and boulder pit in the SLC. Email the listed contact for any further information on how to join!
summary: Baylor Club Climbing offers a welcoming community to those who enjoy climbing and are looking to compete! The team is open to all skill levels whether you are a total beginner or you're sending the hardest routes.

name: Earle Residence Hall - Science & Health LLC
description: The Science & Health Living-Learning Center (S&H-LLC) is a place where students can engage in learning outside of the classroom in a professionally focused community. Students with common interests in pursuing a career in medicine will be able to collaborate with each other as well as science faculty and medical researchers, allowing them to develop the skills and background to become true medical scholars and future leaders in science and healthcare professions. Students immerse themselves in medical and life science experiences that concretely and innovatively connect them with the science faculty and health professionals along with the interests and curriculum of Baylor University. The Science and Health LLC will create opportunities for students to engage medicine and science through research and/or service leadership experiences.
summary: The Science & Health Living-Learning Center (S&H-LLC) is a place where students can engage in learning outside of the classroom in a professionally focused community. Students with common interests in pursuing a career in medicine will be able to colla...

name: Lutherans Doing Life Together
description:
The purpose of this organization is to support each other as a group of Lutheran believers through the development of our relationships with each other and with God, and to intentionally reflect the love of Jesus Christ through outreach beyond the lines of our denomination and service both on the Baylor campus and beyond..."striving side by side for the faith of the gospel". Philippians 1:27

 

We have weekly Bible studies on Monday evenings at 5:30 (food provided), generally in Rogers 207, and social events scheduled 1-2 times per month.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to support each other as a group of Lutheran believers through the development of our relationships with each other and with God, and to intentionally reflect the love of Jesus Christ through outreach beyond the line...

name: Kappa Alpha Order (Fraternity)
description: You come to college with your values intact; you’ve learned from family and friends what’s right and wrong. While in college, KA helps you keep your compass straight, develop your values-set and experiences, and points you in a great direction for your future. Then, with our history and your experience, your journey in life seems a little clearer.
summary: You come to college with your values intact; you’ve learned from family and friends what’s right and wrong. While in college, KA helps you keep your compass straight, develop your values-set and experiences, and points you in a great direction.

name: Martin Residence Hall
description: Martin Hall was built in 1956 and renovated in 2017. The namesake, D.K. Martin (Doc) was born in the late 1870's and graduated from Baylor in 1900. He passed away in 1956, the same year Martin Hall was completed. Martin has gone through many transitions over the years. At one time it had a cafeteria on the first floor, then later a full weight room. It once housed all of the male athletes at Baylor. The most recent renovation is the product of intentional conversation about how community is formed, and how men can come live life-together while reflecting the body of Christ, the Church. Many resources were poured into the newly renovated Martin, and it now stands as one of the premiere Residence Halls among Texas universities.
summary: Martin Hall is home to 250 freshman Baylor men. Martin Hall was built in 1956 and renovated in 2017. The Martin staff seeks to build a deep and rich community where all residents feel rooted and that they have a place to call home.

name: Student Productions
description: The student productions committee is student-led and exists to inspire creativity, foster student leadership, and promote a diverse community while continuing the excellence of traditional Baylor programs. These programs include All-University Sing, Pigskin Revue, After Dark, and Stompfest.
summary: The student productions committee is student-led, and exists to inspire creativity, foster student leadership, and promote a diverse community while continuing the excellence of traditional Baylor programs.

name: InterVarsity Nurses Christian Fellowship at Baylor University
description: Nurses Christian Fellowship (NCF) is both a Christian professional organization and a ministry of and for nurses and nursing students. The purpose of the organization is to establish and advance in nursing, within education and practice, witnessing communities of nursing students and nurse who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God's Word, God's people of every ethnicity and culture and God's purposes in the world.
summary: Nurses Christian Fellowship (NCF) is both a Christian professional organization and a ministry of and for nurses and nursing students.

name: Aero at Baylor
description: Aero at Baylor is a student-led organization that provides students with accelerated hands-on experiences with various aerospace projects. The club competes annually in 3D Printed Aircraft Competition at UTA, in which students gain an understanding of aeronautics and RC electronics through the construction of aircraft. We also compete in IREC (Intercollegiate Rocket Engineering Competition) at Spaceport America. In addition to aerospace projects, Aero provides career-boosting opportunities for students like skill-learning workshops and networking events. We invite anyone interested in what our club has to offer to join and get involved!
summary: Aero at Baylor is a project-based Aerospace Engineering club that welcomes students of all majors to come together to work on amazing projects that allow students to expand their knowledge and skills. This club focuses largely on RC planes and rocketry.

name: Latinx Association of Science and Health
description: The purpose of this organization is to empower Latinx students to go make an impact in their community based on the resources and experiences that we provide to them through membership. LASH strives to increase cultural competency in hopes of inspiring more diversity and inclusivity at Baylor University. In doing so, we support our campus goals towards uplifting underrepresented students and their journey to becoming well rounded health professionals. We take pride in supporting everyone who joins our organization. It is imperative to support each other in this journey on the road to being in health care and science. When you join Lash, you join a familia.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower Latinx students to go make an impact in their community based on the resources and experiences that we provide to them through membership.

name: CURE BU
description: CURE International is a non-profit organization that operates charitable hospitals and programs in 29 countries worldwide where patients experience the life-changing message of God’s love for them, receiving surgical treatment regardless of gender, religion, or ethnicity. CURE BU strives to raise awareness about disabilities in the developing world while providing support for these children through our own communities here at Baylor University and around the Waco community. By hosting fundraisers, we will raise money to sponsor surgical procedures, while also empowering the students and residents of Baylor University to realize that together we can make a difference.
