name: Music & Memory at Baylor University
description: Music & Memory is an organization that seeks to cultivate a community of service, advocacy, and compassion surrounding student volunteers and memory care. We proudly bridge the gap between the Baylor School of Music and Pre-Health Department by bringing a diverse group of students together over a shared love for music therapy and holistic care. We are dedicated to serving our community by bringing personalized music into the lives of individuals diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease and/or dementia. Through monthly music performances and mixers at Living Springs Village, we hope to provide student volunteers with opportunities to foster meaningful connections with memory care residents.
summary: The purpose of Music & Memory is to bring personalized music into the lives of individuals with a wide range of cognitive and physical challenges through digital music technology, vastly improving quality of life. We also put on monthly live music perf...

name: The Country Dance Club at Baylor University
description:
The goal of The Country Dance Club at Baylor University is to promote community involvement through the enjoyment of dancing. It will provide members with the opportunity to meet other students and increase their knowledge of country dancing. Our mission is to provide an educational, engaging, friendly, and inviting experience of country dancing that is inclusive of all Baylor students.

 

Join us on Wednesday evenings from 9-10 in Barfield Drawing Room. Everyone is welcome!
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote the growth of community involvement through the enjoyment of dancing. It will provide an opportunity to meet other students, increase their knowledge of country dancing, and boost physical activity.

name: Delight Ministries
description:
Delight Ministries' mission is to invite college women into Christ-centered community that fosters vulnerability, transforms stories, and leads women to fall deeper in love with Jesus on college campuses, including at Baylor University.

Delight is a place to be fully known, loved, and challenged. We believe in creating community that carries each other’s burdens and celebrates victories. We do this through weekly Bible study meetings, intentional "Delight Dates," and fun events throughout the semester. We believe that one moment with Jesus can truly change everything!

"Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart."
-Psalm 37:4
summary: Delight Ministries is a nationwide ministry that invites college women into Christ-centered community that fosters vulnerability and transforms stories.

name: Association for Women Geoscientists
description: Baylor Association for Women Geoscientists (BAWG) was established on campus in the spring of 2018. The goal of this organization is to provide a supportive and encouraging environment for all members to promote the professional development of women in the geosciences. At Baylor, we hope to achieve these goals through networking, community, and mentorship. Our hallmark event is Her/His Stories that occur throughout the academic year. The purpose of these talks is centered on the idea that every person has a unique story to share and each of us learns from others' personal experiences. We hope to have you join us!
summary: Baylor Association for Women Geoscientists (BAWG) was established in the spring of 2018. Its goal is to provide a supportive and encouraging environment for all members to promote the professional development of women in geoscience related fields.

name: Nutrition Outreach Waco
description:
Nutrition Outreach Waco is a service organization that partners with after-school programs in the Waco area to provide nutrition education to children. We currently volunteer at Highland Baptist Church's after-school program on Friday afternoons. At this site, we provide basic nutrition lessons to the children covering topics such as specific macronutrients, micronutrients, hydration, exercise, etc. We also make a snack for the children for them to better understand what sort of foods relate to the topic we are discussing. 

 

This is a very special organization where you get to gain experience providing nutrition education while also serving the Waco community. It is a good way to gain volunteer hours and looks great on a pre-health, pre-dietetics, or education resume, among others! NOW allows Baylor students to invest in the Waco youth and promote healthy lifestyle behaviors in children. 

 

If you are at all interested in joining our organization, please do not hesitate to reach out to Madison_Kirkwood1@baylor.edu. We welcome members of all majors and classifications who want to serve alongside us!
summary: The purpose of NOW is to unite Baylor students together to invest in the long-term health and wellbeing of Waco’s youth. We especially concentrate on populations at or below the poverty level, through nutrition education and mentoring relationships.

name: Unicycle Academy at Baylor
description: The Unicycle Academy at Baylor is an organization founded to train new members in the art of unicycling. Additionally to create a community for existing unicyclers to excel in their skills! We are a closely-nit group that loves to have fun! We have weekly meetups, weekend rides in parks, group dinners, & we perform in the After Dark Show during Family Weekend & in the Homecoming Parade!!!
summary: The Unicycle Academy at Baylor is an organization founded to train new members in the art of unicycling. Additionally to create a community for existing unicyclers to excel in their skills! We are a closely-nit group that loves to have fun!

name: Videogame Club at Baylor University
description:
The Videogame Club at Baylor University, founded in 2017, is a student organization focused on connecting and bringing together Baylor students by hosting events centered on playing video games. We believe we can form a community of gamers on Baylor University and create an encouraging environment.

We usually hold our events on the second floor of the SUB on campus. We have a club Discord server that we use to communicate as a club, and where we hangout throughout the year. We also have a Steam community page for our PC gamers. Here is the link to the Discord: https://discord.gg/UQWsfgS6un . 

Our club covers most of the basic gaming bases, we have PC, Xbox, Playstation, Nintendo, and retro gamers in the club, so when we have our game day events we typically have setups for everyone to enjoy. If you have any questions about what all we do as a club, feel free to reach out to one of the club's officers.
summary: The Videogame Club at Baylor University, founded in 2017, is a student organization focused on connecting and bringing together Baylor students by hosting events centered on playing video games.

name: Students for Environmental and Wildlife Protection
description: Students for Environmental & Wildlife Protection is an organization that works to promote ecological conservation and appreciation through volunteering, advocating, and raising awareness for our wildlife and environment.
summary: Students for Environmental & Wildlife Protection is an organization that works to promote ecological conservation and appreciation through volunteering, advocating, and raising awareness for our wildlife and environment.

name: Network of Enlightened Women
description: NeW is the nation's premier organization for conservative university women that provides its members with a like-minded community, educational and internship opportunities, and space for intellectual and personal growth! NeW seeks to empower women of all career and professional aspirations and is dedicated to uplifting young conservative women's voices on campus and in the surrounding community.
summary: NeW is a national, non-profit organization for conservative university women! NeW members meet to debate and discuss politics, gender, the media, and conservatism whilst cultivating community and expanding the intellectual diversity on campus.

name: Middle Eastern Student Association at Baylor University
description: The Middle Eastern Student Association (MESA) at Baylor University is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community among Middle Eastern students. Our mission is to celebrate and share the rich cultures, traditions, and experiences of the Middle East with the broader Baylor community. Through a variety of cultural events, engaging socials, and meaningful connections, MESA provides a welcoming space for students to connect, support one another, and take pride in their heritage.
summary: The Middle Eastern Student Association (MESA) at Baylor University is dedicated to fostering a strong sense of community among Middle Eastern students.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated
description: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is a private, not-for-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world. Since its founding more than 200,000 women have joined the organization. The organization is a sisterhood of predominantly Black, college educated women. The sorority currently has 1,000 collegiate and alumnae chapters located in the United States, England, Japan (Tokyo and Okinawa), Germany, the Virgin Islands, Bermuda, the Bahamas, Jamaica and the Republic of Korea
summary: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Incorporated is a private, not-for-profit organization whose purpose is to provide assistance and support through established programs in local communities throughout the world. Since its founding more than 200,000 women hav...

name: Alpha Phi (Sorority)
description: The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 176 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members. Our chapter is home to nearly 300 talented, ambitious and diverse women who strive for excellence in everything they do. Together we are bonded by Alpha Phi’s common set of ideals: sisterhood, a spirit of generosity, high expectations for character and a drive for innovation. In 1872, our founders set the standard for the Alpha Phi of today; a sisterhood that values the past, but looks forward to the progress offered by the future. Each sister is unique and plays a part in our legacy. We strive to be the best versions of ourselves by growing in faith, making philanthropy a driving-force, focusing on academics and being an active part of the local, campus and greek communities.
summary: Alpha Phi is home to ambitious and diverse women who strive for excellence in everything they do. Together we are bonded by Alpha Phi’s common set of ideals: sisterhood, a spirit of generosity, high expectations for character and a drive for innovation.

name: Animal Rescue Club at Baylor University
description: The Animal Rescue Club at Baylor is a group of animal-lovers that strive to rescue animals in Waco, educate the public on proper animal treatment, and volunteer at local animal sanctuaries such as the Pet Circle Regional Animal Center, Waco Wildlife Rescue, and others. We rescue different animals all the way from dogs to kangaroos! Feel free to reach out if you have any questions. Thanks fur checking us out:)
summary: We are here to help students see the benefits of animal rescue, educate the Baylor community on proper care of animals, and volunteer at our local shelter and other community partners!

name: BUILD at Baylor University
description: BUILD at Baylor is a student-led organization that aims to unite the Baylor student body one portable medical clinic at a time. We will start with a shipping container and transform it into this medical clinic.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide medical clinics to a 501 (c) 3 non-profit organization (Medical Bridges) who then sends the medical clinics international to countries in need.

name: Artistic Events
description:
The Creative Arts Experience (CAE) engages students in the fine arts at Baylor by providing enriching programs in art, music, theatre, film, and literature. Students attending CAE’s will:

  • Foster artistic literacy and human empathy by exposing students to a wide variety of creative arts events on the Baylor campus and with Baylor-sponsored programs.
  • Allow for maturation and growth in a student’s appreciation of creative arts events through immersion over an extended period of time.
  • Develop a student’s understanding of the norms of interaction at creative arts events in order to encourage lifelong patronage of the arts.

A&S students, falling under the 2019-2020 catalog and later, shall attend at least 12 CAE events during their time at Baylor in order to graduate, with at least 2 being from each of the 5 event types. BIC students have different requirements. More information, including transfer student waivers, is available at the CAE website and from your advisor.

 

 

CONTENT ADVISORY
The Creative Arts Experience does not offer specific advisories about subject matter, as sensitivities vary from person to person. If you have any questions about content, age-appropriateness or effects (such as strobe lights or theatrical fog) that might have a bearing on patron comfort, please contact the Office of Access and Learning Accommodation here.
summary: The Creative Arts Experience (CAE) engages students in the fine arts at Baylor providing enriching programs of art, music, theatre, film, and literature.

name: International Student Association at Baylor
description:
This organization aims to support international students' academic needs and lifestyle and culture transitions by providing international professors and departmental support on campus and helping create a community for everyone who goes through the same international student journey. 

If you're struggling with College as an international student, TCK, green card holder, or first-generation college student, or if your background might be because you don't have any family member who has been in College in the U.S., please Join us in helping to create a community that supports you!
summary: This organization aims to support International students' academic needs and lifestyle and culture transitions by providing International professor support and helping create a community for everyone who goes through the same international journey.

name: Texas Rising
summary: Texas Rising is a non-partisan, progressive organization dedicated to mobilizing young Texas voters and empowering Baylor students to become involved in politics.

name: Project Sunshine
description: Project Sunshine works with medical centers across the U.S. to transform hospitals, outpatient centers, and long-term care facilities into cheerful spaces for pediatric patients and families. Our trained volunteers deliver fun, creative, and educational programs that bring comfort, hope, and joy during times of medical hardship.
summary: A nonprofit national organization that brings volunteers to pediatric hospitals.

name: Delta Alpha Pi Honor Society
description: This organization celebrates and supports academic achievement, leadership and advocacy for college students with disabilities.
summary: DAPi is an academic honor society founded to recognize high-achieving students with disabilities and organized by students with disabilities.

name: Ping Pong Club at Baylor
description:
We are Baylor Ping Pong club! We bring Baylor students together by developing community through ping pong (table tennis) and other fun activities on campus! We welcome anyone and everyone, and no skill level is needed to join! We however strive to increase the table tennis skills of each and every student and create a space for welcoming others and to build the Baylor community as a whole.

Questions? Contact Davis Gilmore
summary: 5 Year Anniversary, to bring Baylor students together by developing community through ping pong and other fun activities on campus. We welcome all, there is no required skill level to join. However, we do strive to increase everyone's ping pong skills.

name: Bears for a Cure
description:
• Philanthropy
    • BFC will utilize unique methods to collect monetary donations that support our cause, such as tabling, profit shares, and bake sales. Additionally, we’ll also advocate for a cure, raise awareness, and provide education on various forms of cancer and cancer prevention through social media. 
  • Legislative action
    • BFC seeks to get involved with the American Cancer Society Cancer Action Network to help introduce new legislative policy to change the face of cancer. Members are encouraged to get involved by signing petitions, sharing their stories, and learning more about government contracts that fund crucial, cutting edge cancer research.
  • Community
    • When we rally together in the fight against cancer, we can accomplish anything. No one fights alone. BFC serves to create a community of passionate students that have been personally touched by cancer or know someone who has. 
  • Service
    • BFC aims to serve the Baylor community and the greater Waco area through its involvement with non-profit organizations, specifically the American Cancer Society. Members will play a vital role in helping to plan and coordinate our signature Relay for Life event, where we will band together to celebrate survivors, honor those we’ve lost, and fight back against cancer.
summary: Bears for a Cure allows students passionate about cancer an outlet to connect. We serve the Waco community by supporting cancer patients and raising funds for the American Cancer Society and other non-profit organizations.

name: Mind & Motion at Baylor
description:
• M&M creates an affordable opportunity with an inclusive environment for women of any fitness level to explore and find an attainable and enjoyable fitness routine, holistically educating women to better their wellness, and creating community for like-minded women.
  • We hold studio workouts at local fitness studios and gyms, socials, wellness workshops, on campus workouts and more.
  • Membership costs $40 per semester or $70 per year and we hold monthly general meetings throughout the semester. We provide monthly sign-up calendars for events.
  • M&M is a great way to meet women at Baylor who want to live a healthy lifestyle in community!
  • Please visit @mindandmotion.bu on Instagram for more details!
summary: Mind & Motion at Baylor is a fitness and wellness club which strives to provide a positive community for college women to pursue an active lifestyle through workouts, social and wellness events.

name: Missions & Public Life
description:
Missions, Service & Public Life strives to shape Baylor's faithful engagement with Waco and the world by integrating faith, learning, and service. We prepare men and women for worldwide leadership and service with a goal to make a better world...together.

 

To do so, we provide transformational opportunities for students to love their neighbors by cultivating self-discovery, deepening human connection, developing a healthy worldview, and equipping servant-leaders.

 

For more information about Missions & Public Life visit us online.

 

https://youtu.be/1NJN9WmRsgc
summary: Missions, Service & Public Life strives to shape Baylor's faithful engagement with Waco and the world by integrating faith, learning, and service. We prepare men and women for worldwide leadership and service with a goal to make a better world...together

name: Chapel
description:
Chapel is the oldest tradition in American higher education. Baylor believes that the need for members of the academic community to come together to focus on both the God who made them and the universe in which they live has not changed.

When Baylor was founded in 1845, Chapel was a part of life for students and faculty alike. Chapel provides an opportunity for students and faculty to gather on Mondays and Wednesdays at 9:05, 10:10 & 11:15 in Waco Hall to hear from people who excel at what they do as an expression of their commitment to Christ.
summary: As the Baylor community has for over 160 years, we gather most Mondays and Wednesdays of the semester to worship, to engage important issues, and to experience wonderful presentations

name: Student Leadership Development
description:
We believe the process of leadership development is open and accessible to all and should be an integrated element to the learning experience for every student at Baylor. To this end, the Division of Student Life provides opportunities for students to engage in a number of diverse leadership offerings across campus. Regardless of where students first encounter leadership, whether it be through Orientation, Line Camp, intra-murals, or student organizations--their interaction with leadership should be unavoidable, explicitly visible, and oriented toward the common good.

 

Interested in getting involved?  Click the image to start today!

Self-paced, Free to Enroll, Open to all Students

 

 
Earn acknowledgement of leadership experience on your transcript
Connect your involvement to post-graduation goals
Align leadership practice with personal values
    Receive personalized mentoring
summary: Student Leadership Development at Baylor seeks to embolden students towards cultivating the dispositions and practices necessary to faithfully and authentically lead for the common good.

name: Department of Multicultural Affairs
summary: The Department of Multicultural Affairs helps all students on their journey to self-awareness. The department assists students in learning about the many cultures represented on campus, as well as explore opportunities to dialogue across differences.

name: Equity
summary: Baylor University is committed to providing a safe and non-discriminatory learning, living, and working environment for all members of the University community

name: Crochet Club
description: To teach people how to crochet and to create a community of Baylor students that will use their skills to reach out to the larger Waco community.
summary: Learn how to crochet and how to use those skills to help the Waco community through philanthropy.

name: Her Campus Baylor
description:
Her Campus is here to make your life easier while being 100% real with you at all times. Our goals are to encourage, inspire, and celebrate the inspiring women from our past, present, and future. We're basically like, your best friend.

Our chapter's mission is to empower women by being empowered women. We strive to provide a space where women feel comfortable to be who they are and embrace everything that makes them uniquely beautiful.

Whether through our weekly articles or the content we create on our social media, we are constantly engaging in conversations with our community. Our general meetings are held biweekly to give important updates, announce upcoming events, and build community!
summary: Her Campus Baylor is a chapter of Her Campus, the #1 media site for college women worldwide. We strive to empower women through our words, actions and attitudes while engaging the Baylor and Waco community through media content and events.

name: Baylor Women's Rugby Club
description: The Baylor Women's Rugby Club is an organization dedicated to teaching, promoting, encouraging, and understanding the rules and regulations of playing rugby. The club hopes to ensure that all members will find an accepting community of women who can grow together as a team and perform to their best ability.
summary: The Baylor Women’s Rugby Club is aimed towards creating a welcoming community of women with a shared love and passion for the sport of rugby. We welcome players with no experience and hope to teach others about the sport.

name: Bangladeshi Student Association
summary: A community for Bangladeshi Students at Baylor University, Waco, Texas. We aim to build a bond among current students and help the upcoming students with any way possible. We also work to share the rich culture of our country to all Baylor people.

name: Thomistic Institute
description:
Does neuroscience undermine free will or the soul?

Should we legalize physician-assisted suicide?

Is Christianity compatible with evolution?

What can fiction-writers, like J.R.R. Tolkien, Jane Austen, or Flannery O’Connor, tell us about God and how to live?

Should we read the Bible literally?

Do Catholics really believe that the communion wafer is Jesus?

We, the Thomistic Institute, are interested in seeking answers to these and other fundamental questions about reality, the good life, and God. We facilitate such discussions primarily by inviting eminent scholars, experts in their fields, to engage with students about these difficult and important matters. We are inspired by the namesake of the organization – St. Thomas Aquinas – whose vast writings serve as the touchstone for our conversations. In addition to public lectures, we also host small reading groups, where we read and discuss articles from Aquinas’s Summa Theologiae. The Thomistic Institute is an organization based out of the Dominican House of Studies in Washington, D.C., and while we are rooted in the Catholic intellectual tradition, we invite anyone interested in seeking truth to become members and join our conversations.
summary: The Thomistic Institute is a student organization devoted to hosting distinguished speakers and reading groups to pursue discussion of philosophical and theological questions.

name: Global Baylor
description:
Baylor University recognizes that students who are both academically and cross-culturally prepared should be able to adapt and succeed in a diverse and multicultural world. Global Baylor is designed to help students develop globally essential skills and knowledge that will better prepare them for leadership and service in the global society of the 21st century. 

 

The Certificate in Global Engagement is open to all Baylor University undergraduate students, in any major, and will incorporate a mixture of curricular and co-curricular requirements. When completing the certificate program, students will rise through three different levels of Global Engagement, from Entry through Exploration and finally to Engagement.

 

 

Interested in enrolling in the Certificate for Global Engagement?

Send us a message with your name and student ID#. We'll get you set up!

ENROLL HERE!

 

Are you currently enrolled in the Certificate in Global Engagement and are looking for more information about how to log events?

1. Please check out our Step By Step Guide

2. Email us for an in-person walk through: (global_baylor@baylor.edu)
summary: seeks to facilitate efforts of individuals/groups throughout campus to transform the world through international travel and study, the development of greater cultural competency and understanding, and support for an increasingly diverse campus community.

name: Online MBA Program
description: The Baylor Online MBA program is designed for working professionals who desire high education standards with a flexible schedule
summary: The Baylor Online MBA program is designed for working professionals who desire high education standards with a flexible schedule.

name: Association for Supply Chain Management
description: Baylor ASCM is an organization that strives to help current and future supply chain management majors at Baylor. We offer opportunities to educate current and future students about the career, networking opportunities with industry professionals, hands on experience through facility tours and case competitions and opportunities to secure internships and full time positions.
summary: Baylor ASCM exists to enhance students' experience in the supply chain profession by exposing students to professional development opportunities which provide expertise on the ever-developing industry.

name: Bears Disc Golf Club
description: Welcome to the Disc Golf Club. The goal of the club is to get more people in the Baylor community involved with disc golf casually or competitively.
summary: Welcome to the Baylor Disc Golf Club. The goal of the club is to get more people in the Baylor community involved with disc golf and to help players improve. We plan on meeting weekly at Cameron Park.

name: Graphic Design Student Association
description: We wish to foster more conversations and preparation for young design students and future design professionals through a variety of activities and events that will encourage, improve, and solidify our talents and our passions beyond the classroom and within the design industry.
summary: A professional development organization for students pursuing careers in graphic design

name: Hawai'i Club
description: As the Hawai'i Club, we hope to educate others within Baylor and Waco about the vibrant cultures and traditions of the Hawaiian Islands, host activities and events that spread the spirit of aloha, and provide opportunities for students to build positive relationships and get involved as leaders. As more people from Hawai'i join the Baylor Family each year, we feel that we have the great opportunity and responsibility to share what our islands have to offer, while being a community that brings a little part of home to our Hawai'i-grown students at Baylor. We hope you’ll join our ‘ohana, no matter where you are from! Mahalo (thank you!)
summary: The Hawai'i Club is here to share and celebrate the Hawaiian culture and Aloha spirit with the Baylor community! Join our 'ohana to learn more about the islands, participate in different traditions, and build meaningful friendships!

name: Baylor Undergraduate Law Review
description: The Baylor Undergraduate Law Review (BULR) gives members the experience of being a part of a law review, while also cultivating awareness of current legal issues. Our organization aims to improve legal studies and understanding for all students while providing a forum to develop skills essential to legal scholarship.
summary: The Baylor Undergraduate Law Review writes, edits, and publishes law review journals with articles submitted by fellow undergraduate students.

name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The National Society of Leadership and Success (NSLS) is the largest accredited collegiate leadership honor society in the United States, with over 2 million members across 800+ college and university chapters.

The NSLS provides a structured leadership development program designed to help students discover and achieve their personal and professional goals. Members gain access to exclusive benefits, including professional networking opportunities, leadership training, and over $400,000 in scholarships annually.

Through its mission of “Building Leaders Who Make a Better World,” the NSLS empowers members to develop the skills, confidence, and connections necessary to create meaningful change.
summary: The NSLS, the nation’s largest accredited leadership program, has 2M+ members across 800 campuses. It offers leadership training, networking, and $400K+ in scholarships to help students grow as leaders.

name: Oso Stoked Surf Club
description: Oso Stoked Surf Club is a Baylor organization that promotes surfing, community, and engagement. OSSC's purpose is to inspire potential and experienced surfers to come together in a team setting to enjoy the sport of surfing. We encourage our members surfing progress, cultivating friendships, and working together as a team.
summary: Club that teaches students how to surf and builds community via outreach and service.

name: Turning Point USA at Baylor
description: Turning Point USA is a non-profit organization that seeks to educate students about the importance of freedom, free markets, and limited government, identify student activists across the country who believe in limited government and individual liberty, and organize young activists in chapters and networks across the country.
summary: Turning Point USA at Baylor aims to create a community through meetings, socials, speakers, and more.

name: Sports Analytics Club
description: We create opportunities for students to work with real-world data in the field of sports. We will perform data visualization and data analysis through statistical methods with the data supplied at collegiate and/or professional levels.
summary: The Sports Analytics Club focuses on (1) discussion of topics in sports from a statistical framework, (2) collaboration on projects involving data from Baylor, and (3) preparing students for work in an data analytics position for a sports company.

name: Theatre Student Society
description:
1. The mission statement of the Baylor University Theatre Student Society Executive Council is to benefit the Baylor University Department of Theatre Arts by:
    1. Representing Members of the Society to the University, faculty, staff, administration and student body at large.
    2. Promoting high standards of professionalism in theatrical productions.
    3. Nurturing the commitment of theatre majors in the department.
    4. Encouraging exceptional creativity and artistry.
    5. Fostering mentor relationships.
  2. Scholarship Fund Mission: Support individuals in reaching these goals by providing scholarship funds for Baylor Theatre Scholarships.
summary: The Baylor University Theatre Student Society aims to support and benefit the Department of Theatre Arts in various ways, while also providing exciting events for theatre students.

name: Community Engagement and Service
description:
In keeping with Baylor’s Christian mission to educate men and women for worldwide leadership and service, the Academy seeks to connect faculty, staff, and students with the surrounding community through informed engagement, civic obligation, and impactful service in order to promote positive social change.  Baylor's mission inextricably links leadership and service and the following opportunities seek to live out that mission in tangible ways. 

In our commitment to Christ and the work with each other as well as with the community at large, we will be:

  • Empowering in all that we do, we seek to build capacity and to foster human capability
  • Purposeful as we work with the goal of having a positive impact. We look for ways to assess that impact and to improve our efforts.
  • Caring and focus on compassion, responsibility, and stewardship.
  • Collaborative as we build trust and effective working relationships internally and externally through listening and cooperation.
summary: CES seeks to connect faculty, staff, and students with the surrounding community through informed engagement, civic obligation, and impactful service in order to promote positive social change.

name: Healthcare is Ours
description:
Healthcare is Ours is an organization tailored to bring diversity to the healthcare industry
through mentorship. Our mission is to mentor and inform the students of Waco ISD of available
resources to assist the students to reach the next level in their academic journey.
summary:
Healthcare is Ours is a mentorship program that aims to increase diversity in healthcare by
encouraging and equipping minorities in Waco ISD.

name: Medlife
description: MEDLIFE is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development projects. In order to achieve the ultimate goal of a world free from the constraints of poverty, we empower students and volunteers to become advocates for change by creating on-campus Chapters, volunteering in Service Learning Trips, and supporting the Moving Mountains giving program. With this support, MEDLIFE hires and trains local staff to provide high-quality 24/7/365 patient care, community development projects, and educational workshops to empower communities in need.
summary: MEDLIFE is a  non-profit organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development projects.

name: A Moment of Magic
description:
2025-2026 Membership Form

2024-2025 Executive Board Application (new members welcome to apply!)

 

AMoM is a service organization with the mission to improve the quality of life of vulnerable and underserved children and inspire them to be brave, strong, and fearless through fun and engaging social wellness activities. We accomplish this through creative programming that promotes social-emotional wellness and coping with daily life stressors exacerbated by a child and family’s circumstances. 

Our most popular services include Tailored Bedside Visits, Character Visits, Athlete Visits, Creative Arts Workshops, Mental Health Workshops, Direct Family Support, and Inter-family Connection. Our partnerships through our national foundation and university chapters span children’s hospitals, community nonprofits, CureFest, and many more organizations benefiting underserved populations. You can read more about us and our mission here: HOME | A MOMENT OF MAGIC 

As a Baylor Chapter, we have closely partnered with Mission Waco to host weekly arts and craft classes and planned Character Visits as part of our creative programming. This upcoming fall we have formed partnerships with Dallas Children's Health, McLane Children's, Cook Children's, Dell Children's Medical Center, and local nonprofits. College volunteers will have access to AMoM's honor society, Alpha Nu Zeta, and many other special leadership and internship opportunities through Baylor's chapter and the national foundation. Members can serve as Magic Maker volunteers or train to become a beloved Character for our visits! We welcome all students into our supportive community for weekly chapter meetings, socials, and service events.
summary: A Moment of Magic is a chapter of the 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that improves the quality of life of vulnerable and underserved children and inspires them to be brave, strong, and fearless through fun and engaging social wellness activities.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description:
The purpose of this organization is to bring and promote Christ-centered missional life to the body of healthcare professionals, students, and its partners. We hope to foster relationships between students, aspiring professionals, and current professionals in, but not limited to medicine, law, business, education, and engineering.

The core values of the organization consist of four components:

  • 1. Help local churches to fulfill medical missions work around the world through connecting, teaching, and training.
  • 2. Empower the next generation of healthcare professionals to advance into the medical mission field through mentoring, training, supporting, and providing mission opportunities.
  • 3. Awaken mission-minded students and healthcare professionals to fulfill the great commission through networking and partnering.
  • 4. Link students and healthcare professionals with global mission organizations.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to bring and promote Christ-centered missional life to the body of healthcare professionals, students, and its partners.

name: Ice Hockey Club
description: The official Baylor Ice Hockey team. We play in the ACHA Divison II league, in the TCHC conference against 7 other Texas teams. These teams consist of TCU, UT, A&M, TXST, ETBU, SMU, and Houston. We also play some non-conference games against teams like UNT, DBU, Texas Tech, and other teams in the surrounding states. As a new organization on campus, we are hoping to make a playoff run and qualify for regionals in the 25-26 season, and our long-term goal for Waco is to bring an ice hockey rink within the city. We are primarily student-funded and are constantly looking for donors and sponsorships from those looking to support the team.
summary: The official Baylor Ice Hockey team page. Tryouts for the 25-26 season will be held on Friday, September 4. Please fill out the interest form located on our website.

name: Koinonia InterVarsity
description:
Baylor Koinonia InterVarsity (KIV) is an organization that builds a community centered on following Jesus and loving our neighbor. Our mission is: Welcoming all students to belong, believe, and become like Jesus.

In Bible studies, around meals, and in everyday life, we strive to develop our relationships with God and with one another in Christ. We have multiple Bible studies that meet weekly, large groups that meet on Fridays in Elliston Chapel, and other fun events that happen periodically throughout the semester. As a part of InterVarsity, a national campus ministry organization, it is our mission to bring the love of Jesus Christ to every corner of campus.
summary: KIV seeks to love Baylor's campus by welcoming all students to belong, believe, and become like Jesus.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
description: Academic Organization focused on providing resources and professional development for students pursuing a career in the industry of Risk Management and Insurance
summary: .Academic Organization focused on providing resources and professional development for students pursuing a career in the industry of Risk Management and Insurance

name: Caribbean Student Association
description:
1. Promote understanding of the development, cultures, and heritage of the Caribbean from a historical context, while fostering curiosity and passion for learning about other cultures.
  2. Encourage students to come out of their comfort zone by experiencing cultures of the Caribbean and understanding how culture influences one’s view of the larger world.
  3. Provide effective leadership opportunities through events and programs, to assist Baylor students to act in an informed and ethical manner in our global society.
  4. To cultivate the experiences of Baylor students through mentorship, service learning, domestic and international study, and enhance their social capital through networking, and professional growth.
  5. Encourage a commitment to excellence in the daily operation of The Caribbean Student Association and uphold its members to the standard of excellence and dedication to the organization’s mission.
summary: To foster a community where Caribbean students are able to share their unique culture, support one another academically, socially and culturally, and serve as an information and support center for all students with primary focus on Caribbean origins.

name: Student Occupational Therapy Association
description:
The purpose shall be to enhance the knowledge of the profession of occupational therapy through professional, educational, community, and social activities. The organization will facilitate opportunities for camaraderie among students, as well as provide meaningful, productive interaction with faculty. As SOTA at Baylor, we commit to aligning our performance as students with the mission of the entry-level Doctor of Occupational Therapy’s mission to prepare practice scholars, educational innovators, and professional leaders who utilize clinically meaningful research in the implementation of best practices to meet the changing demands of the Occupational Therapy profession.

SOTA @ Baylor's Primary Goals:

To promote the profession of occupational therapy through professional, educational, community, and social activities.

To act as a liaison between the Doctor of Occupational Therapy students at Baylor University and the faculty and staff members at Baylor University.

To act as a liaison between the Doctor of Occupational Therapy students at Baylor University and the American Occupational Therapy Association (AOTA).

To do our part as student leaders to assist members in their endeavors to develop competence in the curricular threads of the entry-level Doctor of Occupational Therapy program: Clinical Reasoning Process, Occupation-Focused View of Humanity, Client-Centered Service Delivery, Servant Leadership, and Scholarly Practice.

To cultivate extraordinary relationships by providing meaningful opportunities for servant leadership in Waco, local communities, state occupational therapy associations, and within our national AOTA organization.
summary: SOTA @ Baylor purposes to enhance the knowledge of the profession of occupational therapy through servant leadership, advocacy, and scholarship.

name: Students for Environmental Equity
description: The mission of Students for Environmental Equity (SEE) is to promote environmental health/public health equity on the state and national level by uniting students in an endeavor to change perspectives and policy. Our overall purpose is two-fold: to educate the student body, and to mobilize students to take action on important environmental health issues through advocacy and lobbying. These environmental health issues include air/water pollution, climate change, toxic chemical contamination, tropical diseases, etc. We seek to foster an interdisciplinary approach and are open to all students, including those on the pre-med, pre-law, and pre-grad tracks.
summary: Interested in Environmental Advocacy? Students for Environmental Equity (SEE) is an organization at Baylor dedicated to educating the student body on important public health/environmental justice issues and mobilizing students to take action.

name: Finance Society
description:
The Baylor Finance and Investment Club is a selective, student-led community preparing undergraduates for roles across high finance. Our mission is to provide mentorship, industry connections, and competitive internship and full-time offers across Tier 1 cities.

The club blends a dual focus on markets and career development, with members actively managing a portfolio while delivering stock pitches and regular market updates, helping them build financial literacy, investment analysis skills, and presentation expertise. Mentorship is hands-on and covers the full recruitment cycle: securing your first internship, building your network, preparing for interviews, and eventually landing the job. Sessions are led by students with offers across high finance, alongside professionals already in the industry. We also host speakers from investment banking, private equity, hedge funds, and other asset classes.

The club aims to be a tight-knit and synergetic group of high performers whose skills will develop through close work with mentors and each other. We target plac
summary:
The Baylor Finance and Investment Club is centered around the following two pillars: 
1. Career mentorship for students interested in High Finance.
2. Hands-on financial markets experience.

name: Poetry Club
description: We are a club that aims to create a safe place for all members, regardless of background, major, or writing experience, to express themselves through poetry. We have time to share our own or others' poetry, learn from great poets and professors, participate in workshops, and more. We are always excited to welcome new members and learn together, and look forward to seeing you soon!
summary: Our mission is to create a safe space to explore the human experience through the creative medium of poetry. This is a place to learn about poetry, express yourself using it, and gain new ways and techniques to do so.

name: Startup and Innovation Club
description: The Startup and Innovation Club empowers student entrepreneurs with resources, education, and real-world experience. Join a driven community in an incubator-style setting with direct access to professors, focused time for your ideas, and connections to Waco’s entrepreneurial network. Whether you’re a business major or just curious, we want you! Be there for every event as it keeps you curious and a drive to keep learning. Be inspired. Take action.
summary: Providing student creatives with the resources to build a strong foundation for their entrepreneurial endeavors.

name: Pickleball Club
description:
The Baylor Pickleball Club is a club open to players of all skill ranges. Whether you're picking up a paddle for the first time or the hundredth time this is the club for you! The club contains a mix of casual and competitive play and encourages daily pick-up games. The Baylor Pickle-Ball Club's first and most important goal is to have fun and we strive to foster community. 

1 Peter 4:8-11
Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins. Offer hospitality to one another without grumbling. Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as faithful stewards of God’s grace in its various forms

GroupMe
summary: To connect people together through the median of pickleball. We strive to create community while improving and fine-tuning our pickle-ball skills.

name: Student Mobilization
description: As an interdenominational ministry, our desire is to help students experience God in a genuine way and to grow in that relationship during this season of life and beyond. We seek to help build leaders that will have a positive impact on their families, communities, country and the world. Our desire is to help shape and develop the character of students in a way that will help serve and influence them the rest of their lives.
summary: Our purpose is to build spiritual leaders for Christ from the college campuses of the world.

name: Multicultural Student Nurses' Association
description:
The Multicultural Student Nurses’ Association (MSNA) was chartered by Baylor University in 2022 in response to student need for an organization that could represent the interests of prenursing and nursing students from diverse cultural backgrounds.  By coordinating monthly meetings, fundraisers, service events, and other activities, MSNA seeks to  

  • enrich the academic experience of our multicultural student population 
  • unify students through common interests 
  • foster the professional role and leadership development of our members 
  • raise awareness about the health disparities in our diverse cultural communities  
  • promote the provision of culturally congruent nursing care 
  • provide opportunities to network with nursing leaders from diverse cultural backgrounds.
summary: The Multicultural Student Nurses’ Association (MSNA) was chartered by Baylor University in 2022 in response to student need for an organization that could represent the interests of prenursing and nursing students from diverse cultural backgrounds.

name: Bears Helping Hearts
description:
Bears Helping Hearts (BHH) is a nursing student organization open to any nursing student who is interested in learning about congenital heart disease and defects. The student should also seek to understand and help create awareness of the mission of The Children’s Heart Foundation (CHF): to advance the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of congenital heart defects (CHDs) by funding the most promising research. BHH nursing students will help support CHF by planning and joining events that will create awareness about CHD to family members, friends, and within the nursing community. These events include and are not limited to toy-drives, fundraisers, hospital visits (when possible), speaker events, and walks. The organization serves the following purposes:

  • Congenital heart disease education for nursing students
  • Serve the congenital heart disease community through volunteer service
  • Raise awareness of congenital heart disease through events
  • Provide leadership opportunities to nursing students
summary: Bears Helping Hearts (BHH) is open to any nursing student who is interested in learning about congenital heart disease & defects while raising awareness of it and supporting action to treat and prevent it.

name: Astronomy Club
summary: Astronomy club members will explore meteors, asteroids, satellites, planets, stars, galaxies and much more! At the monthly meeting, members will discover the grandeur of the cosmos by using telescopes, binoculars, and the naked eye.

name: Ducks Unlimited at Baylor
description:
Our mission: Ducks Unlimited at Baylor conserves, restores and manages wetlands and associated habitats for North America’s waterfowl. These habitats also benefit other wildlife and people.

Why we need you: We want to bring a wildlife conservation organization to Baylor University and it all starts with a passion for the outdoors and hunting. We need to spread the importance of conservation to future generations. We also need your passion for waterfowl conservation and the outdoors to carry out our mission. It all starts with you!

How this will benefit you: You will make many connections with people who share the same passion for the outdoors as you do. You will be involved in protecting waterfowl for future generations, gaining experience that will go beyond this organization, and the opportunity to be involved in a unique niche here at Baylor University. Lastly, you are given the opportunity to participate in leadership.
summary: This club helps restore and manage wetlands to help benefit wildlife and people.

name: Lebanese Student Association
description: The association is committed to establishing a voice for the Lebanese, and Lebanese-American students, while, promoting Lebanese culture and heritage, thus adding to the diversity of the campus as well as increasing public awareness about Lebanon.
summary: Lebanese Student Association is an organization aiming to unite and serve the Lebanese community on the Baylor campus while educating the general student body about Lebanese culture and society.

name: Campus Girl Scouts
description: Campus Girl Scouts is a student-led organization promoting leadership and community service. Most members are former Girl Scouts, however, anyone can join.
summary: Through volunteering and personal leadership growth, Baylor Campus Girl Scouts builds a community of women who propel the global Girl Scout Movement forward and make the world a better place.

name: Adventist Christian Fellowship
description: ACF is about the mission of God through the Seventh-day Adventist Church on college and university campuses in the North America Division including Bermuda, Canada, Guam-Micronesia and the United States. ACF is a ministry of students, by students and for students, supported by local Adventist churches and resourced by Adventist Conferences, Unions and the North American Division. It includes concerned parents, faculty, pastors, chaplains, church leaders, church members and hundreds of volunteers. All this because when we reach the campus we help change the world!
summary: ACF is about the mission of God through the Seventh-day Adventist Church on college campuses. Baylor ACF is a ministry of students, by students, and for students. Baylor ACF is a Christian community that gets together in Bible study and fellowship.

name: Students Demand Action
description: • Students Demand Action is a national movement of young activists committed to ending gun violence. We advocate for common sense gun laws, emphasize gun safety, and provide mental health resources. name: Lambda Pi Eta
description:
Lambda Pi Eta (LPH) is the National Communication Association’s official honor society for four-year colleges and universities. The purpose of LPH is to promote outstanding academic achievement in the Communication discipline. Membership is open to Communication majors, minors, and graduate students. 
 
To be eligible for membership as an undergraduate student, you must:
  • Be a Communication major or minor
  • Be in good standing (i.e., not on academic notice)
  • Have earned 60 hours with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0
  • Have earned 12 hours in CSS courses with a minimum GPA of 3.25 for all CSS courses
To be eligible for membership as a graduate student, you must:
  • Be a Communication graduate student
  • Be enrolled as a student in good standing (i.e., not on academic probation)
  • Have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.50
  • Have earned 12 hours in CSS graduate coursework
summary: Lambda Pi Eta (LPH) is the National Communication Association’s official honor society for four-year colleges and universities. The purpose of LPH is to promote outstanding academic achievement in the Communication discipline.

name: Medical Anthropology Society
description: The Medical Anthropology Society is an undergraduate and graduate student organization which calls attention to the overlap between medical practice and anthropology. Through collaboration with the Baylor Pre-Health community, we seek to explore the roles which cultural, historical, and political factors contribute to the health and treatment of patients. Through monthly informative meetings, mentorship from anthropology graduate students, and journal club, we hope to cultivate knowledge and growth for students with the medical anthropology field, especially in the local Waco context.
summary: This society aims to provide a supportive and collaborative environment for undergraduate and graduate students, promoting medical anthropology.

name: Warfighter Weightlifting Club
summary: The purpose of the Warfighter Weightlifting Club is to provide a unique avenue for fitness here at Baylor, to provide members an insight into ROTC programs here on campus, and to promote a culture of fitness and personal growth for all those involved.

name: Minorities in Medicine at Baylor University
description: The Minorities in Medicine Organization at Baylor University is a Pre-Health Organization. The primary purpose of this organization is to advocate and increase representation & equality for minorities in medicine. Additionally, the club will serve to promote and foster unity within the Pre- Health community at Baylor, provide academic resources to apply to professional school utilizing scholarships & mentorship, while also providing academic and community enrichment such as clinical skills workshops, group discussions, social events, and community service.
summary: Minorities In Medicine at Baylor University is a Pre-Health organization that holds many purposes and goals.

name: Pi Epsilon
description:
To be eligible as a regular member of the Society, students must be enrolled in an interdisciplinary environmental science program at an accredited institution where a chapter of Pi Epsilon is established. If the institution does not offer an interdisciplinary environmental science program, students must be enrolled in a natural science program at an accredited institution where a chapter of Pi Epsilon is established. Undergraduates and graduates may apply for membership if they fulfill the following criteria:

 

Undergraduates must:

  1. Have completed at least 36 semester hours (54 quarter hours) of coursework with a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.30.
  2. Have completed at least three quarter- or semester-length courses in environmental science, of which at least one is not an introductory course, with cumulative grade point average of 3.30 out of 4.00 or its equivalent in those environmental science courses.
  3. Be in good academic standing.

If the institution does not offer an interdisciplinary environmental science program, course of study must consist of at least two of the natural science disciplines, with coursework in one discipline not to exceed 75% of the total science coursework.

  1. Natural science courses must include courses in at least two of the four following disciplines: biology, chemistry, geology, and physics.

Graduate students shall:

  1. Have completed at least 8 semester hours (12 quarter hours) of graduate level environmental science coursework.
  2. Maintain a cumulative grade point average of at least 3.30.
  3. If the institution does not offer an interdisciplinary environmental science program, course of study must consist of at least two of the four natural science disciplines (biology, chemistry, geology, and physics) with coursework in one discipline not to exceed 75% of the total science coursework.

Students will be invited to join Pi Epsilon as a member if they meet the criteria as listed above. Please reach out to julie_king@baylor.edu and erica_bruce@baylor.edu and/or marlo_treece1@baylor.edu for more information on the necessary qualifications for membership eligibility.
summary: The purpose of the Society is to promote the study of environmental sciences through recognition of exemplary scholarly and professional activity.

name: Bears With T1D
summary: This organization is designed to be a support system for individuals battling Type 1 Diabetes on Baylor's campus. We come together in order to create lifelong bonds through social events and community.

name: SACNAS at Baylor
description: SACNAS at Baylor is the local chapter of Society for the Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in STEM. As a multicultural and multidisciplinary organization dedicated to advancing diversity in STEM fields, we empower students by providing professional development opportunities, fostering cultural individualism, and encouraging authenticity in academic and social spaces. Through advocacy and community engagement, we support the growth of future leaders who will flourish both personally and professionally.
summary: SACNAS at Baylor an inclusive source of community with the mission of supporting underrepresented minorities in attaining advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in STEM.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc.
description:
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college.

On the evening of November 17, 1911, Omega Psi Phi was founded inside the Science Building (later renamed Thirkield Hall) at Howard University located in Washington, D.C. The founders were three undergraduates — Edgar Amos Love, Oscar James Cooper and Frank Coleman. Joining them was their faculty adviser, Ernest Everett Just.

The initial effort to develop the membership potential for a possible chapter of the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity at Baylor University in Waco, Texas occurred with the initiation of Bro. Larry B. Lipscomb who was the first undergraduate brother from Baylor University to be initiated in the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity.The Supreme Council of the Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc., officially approved the chartering of the Chi Delta Delta Chapter for Baylor University in Waco, Texas on December 5, 1998.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college. On the evening of November 17, 1911, Omega Psi Phi was founded at Howard University.

name: Global Health Student Network
description: GHSN is an interdisciplinary community that promotes sustainable approaches to address the root causes of global healthcare disparities through a culturally-sensitive, holistic lens. We aim to cultivate the next generation of global health ambassadors through partnership, service, research, and advocacy.
summary:
The purpose of GHSN is to promote global healthcare equity on the domestic and international stage by uniting students through global missions and global health dialogue. 

Follow our Instagram: @baylorglobalhealth
Contact us: baylorghsn@gmail.com

name: Powerlifting Club at Baylor University
description: The Powerlifting Club at Baylor is an organization of enthusiasts that are dedicated to the lifelong sport of powerlifting. We are an all inclusive club, catering to athletes of all skill levels. This organization strives to be an educational resource, as well as a way for students to begin or further their fitness journeys so that they may leave Baylor's campus with the skills and knowledge needed to live a life of healthy habits.
summary: This new powerlifting organization would seek to bring education about the sport, proper training guidance, and a close-knit community to individuals both interested in and actively practicing the discipline of powerlifting.

name: Fireside Fellowship
description: The purpose of Fireside Fellowship is to build up an inclusive Christian community by studying the Word of God and developing a judgment-free environment based on mutuality and faith. We foster fellowship and discipleship on campus and in our community by organizing Bible studies and reading through the New Testament in its entirety each year. Through the involvement of faculty, staff and local Waco families, we hope to provide opportunities for mentorship for our members. We believe that through such practices we will grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ (2 Peter 3:18). C@B will be intentional in reaching out to all Christians at Baylor who desire to worship, have fellowship, learn and grow in their faith regardless of denomination or where they meet for church. Fireside Fellowship will also be intentional in presenting the gospel of Christ to any student who may not be a Christian but would like to be one.

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Scholars of Finance is a rapidly-growing national organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. The National Organization invests in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and a life-long support community of peers, alumni, and purpose-driven investors.

 

Finance provides a supportive environment for individuals to connect, collaborate, and learn from one another. The organization's core mission extends beyond technical proficiency, placing equal emphasis on developing foundational leadership skills and facilitating comprehensive professional development. By combining these essential elements, Scholars of Finance equips its members with the knowledge, values, and connections necessary to become effective stewards of change, driving the finance industry towards a brighter and more ethically conscious future.
summary: This innovative community is dedicated to fostering a new generation of finance professionals who prioritize ethics, integrity, and social responsibility.

The Student Food Security Council will also partner with other students and Baylor departments to better understand and destigmatize college student food insecurity, while exploring hunger solutions not currently being implemented on campus. 

Participation on the Student Food Security Council is voluntary. The membership application will be posted as availability opens up, so stay in the loop!

name: Trap and Skeet at Baylor
description:
The Baylor Trap and Skeet team focus on educating our members on the safety precautions and rules of shotgun sports. These sports include, but are not limited to, Trap, Skeet, and Sporting Clays. As a competitive entity, the team’s goal is to participate in regional, national, and potentially international shooting competitions. However we aim to be a club open to anyone interested in the sport, no matter their skill level. Beyond competition, the team functions as a platform for skill development, we offer regular practice sessions and tournament opportunities to help our members refine and grow their skills. In addition to its competitive and social aspects, the Baylor Trap and Skeet team plays a role in promoting the sport within the university and the wider community. Overall, the team provides a structured and disciplined setting for individuals to engage in the recreational, physically demanding, and skill-enhancing activities of our sport while representing the university and contributing to the broader promotion of shooting sports on the collegiate level.

Band link: https://band.us/n/a4a1beSay2L6V
summary: The Baylor Trap and Skeet team focus on educating our members on the safety precautions and rules of shotgun sports. These sports include, but are not limited to, Trap, Skeet, and Sporting Clays.

name: Open Table
description:
Do you have questions and uncertainties about faith? Do you want to learn how faith is connected to justice and compassion for the marginalized? Do you like eating food?

Open Table centers around three principles:

1) Community is essential for all people.

2) Being a person of faith does not mean that you are always certain of what you believe.

3) Something special happens when diverse people sit at a common table and eat together.

We gather weekly to eat together and have conversations about faith and community, giving special attention to the experiences of those often marginalized by faith institutions. Some weeks we discuss Scripture and theology. Other weeks we create art or explore unique, embodied forms of prayer and worship.
summary: Open Table is a campus ministry that exists to provide a community of hospitality for students who are seeking to explore faith.

description: Letters of Love is an international nonprofit organization that lends emotional support to children in hospitals through the creation of handwritten cards. Inspired by the power of small acts of love, Letters of Love (abbreviated as LOL) chapters across the world have sent over 200,000 cards to children in the hospital. Believing in the power of hope, cheesy puns, fun drawings, and the simplicity of compassion, the Baylor chapter is dedicated to supporting youth from our campus.
summary: Letters of Love is an international nonprofit organization that lends emotional support to children in hospitals through the creation of handwritten cards. The Baylor chapter seeks to uphold this mission with joy, encouragement, and lots of cards!

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon Honor Society
description:
Becoming a member of Upsilon Pi Epsilon (UPE) is more than just an honor—it’s an opportunity to be recognized among the best and brightest in computing and technology!

UPE is the only international honor society exclusively for computing and information disciplines, with a rich history of promoting academic excellence, leadership, and service. We are proudly affiliated with prestigious organizations like the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) and the IEEE Computer Society, connecting you to a global network of top professionals and researchers.

As a member, you will gain

  • A low commitment high reward experience - Membership is free, and the time commitment is minimal!
  • Scholarship access from dedicated UPE/ACM funds
  • Opportunities to mentor underclassmen in their academic pursuits
  • An additional graduation cord (for free) to honor your membership with UPE
  • Recognition as one of the top students in your class!
summary: UPE is the only international honor society exclusively for computing and information disciplines and is proudly affiliated with prestigious organizations such as the ACM and IEEE. Only the top students from each class are invited to become members.

name: Young Americans for Freedom/Baylor Chapter
description: Learn more about the conservative movement and politics in America by joining the Baylor YAF Chapter and attending our meetings and events throughout the semester. Challenge leftist ideals and meet fellow conservatives while hearing from speakers and participating in fun, informative, and exciting events!
summary: YAF is a politically conservative organization that advocates for individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional American values.

name: BearAid
description:
The BearAid Team provides:

Relief Response – The team will travel after disasters strike to provide immediate relief through activities such as cleaning up trash and debris, sorting and distributing supplies at distribution centers, cooking and feeding both those affected by the disaster and the volunteers who come to help, and various other tasks that help neighbors begin the process of recovery. These trips will often occur within 2-3 weeks of the disaster on weekends, after locations are vetted by partner organizations and tasks are available that our team can do safely.

Fire response –The team may be called out to assist firefighters and first responders at major fires by providing hydration and food, checking/triaging responders as they come off the field, and meeting other needs based on the situation, such as cleanup or meals. These trips will occur as the disaster is occurring, and the team will be positioned in safe staging areas for the first responders.

Local Response – The team will be ready to respond as the need arises in our own community, such as a hydration and cooling station at home football games. These trips will occur as needed in our area.

Recovery Response – The team is invited to participate in the long-term recovery of carefully selected locations that Baylor Missions partners with. Through this, the team will work alongside those in the community who are leading rebuilding efforts to help move through the recovery phase. This may include tasks more aligned to rebuilding efforts, such as dry-walling and painting. These trips will often occur during scheduled class breaks, such as Winter break, Spring Break, and in May after graduation.
summary: When natural disaster strikes, the BearAid Team serves communities throughout the relief and recovery process by participating in transformational activities of service that deepen a love for neighbors.

name: Pre-Health Brigades at Baylor
description: Our organization focuses on recruiting students to participate in Medical Brigades, a program that allows students to take part in international service projects. The goal is to give students the chance to help improve healthcare and development in communities that need it most. While working on these projects, students also develop important skills like leadership, teamwork, and cultural awareness. By joining, students can make a real difference while learning how to be responsible, global citizens and gain experience that will help them in their future studies and careers.
summary: Our organization focuses on recruiting students to attend Medical Brigades. The primary purpose is to empower students to engage in impactful international developmental work while fostering global citizenship and leadership skills.

name: Track Club at Baylor
summary: This club is for Baylor students who enjoy participating in Track and Field events.

name: American Forum

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) at Baylor University

The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) at Baylor University is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for Hispanic and Latino engineers and scientists. Our mission is to empower our members—academically, professionally, and personally—by creating a space where cultural identity and academic ambition thrive together.

We are committed to serving as a catalyst in the careers and futures of our members by providing meaningful networking opportunities with leading companies and top-tier graduate programs. Through company visits, career development events, and mentorship, we help our members build strong professional foundations and open doors to impactful opportunities.

Above all, SHPE at Baylor emphasizes collaboration. We believe in the power of unity and shared experiences, which is why we actively seek to build strong relationships with other Hispanic/Latino organizations on campus and connect with SHPE chapters across Texas. Whether through joint socials, service projects, or conferences, we are dedicated to strengthening our community and growing together.
summary: The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) at Baylor University is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for Hispanic and Latino engineers.

name: Heartfire at Baylor University
description: The overall goal we hope to achieve through this organization is to ignite a passion among Baylor students for local, national, and international Christian evangelical missions. The foundations and pillars of the Heartfire Club will be based upon various scriptures that call believers to go and live on missions: Matthew 28:19, Jeremiah 20:9, Acts 1:8, 1 Corinthians 9:16. Among our members, we will make the last commandment by Jesus our first priority and uphold that across all of campus and beyond. Through our partnership with the medical missions non-profit organization Heartfire Missions, we hope to connect our members with opportunities to discover, pray for, and find some capacity to reach unreached people groups. We will measure our success in achieving these goals by the attendance of our members at monthly general meetings, biweekly prayer groups, and then by the number of those volunteering for local and international ministries.
summary: The specific purpose of this organization will be to ignite and foster a passion within Baylor students for missions.
