name: Pentecost Campus Ministry at Baylor University
description:
Pentecost Campus Ministry is a vibrant community of students and professionals passionate about knowing Jesus Christ and making Him known. Our mission is to disciple, equip, and empower students to grow spiritually, excel academically, and lead purpose-driven lives.

Through Bible studies, prayer meetings, mentorship, evangelism, and community outreach, we create a supportive environment where faith and excellence thrive together.

Whether you’re seeking to strengthen your walk with God, develop leadership skills, or find a faith-filled family on campus, you’re welcome here.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to bring students to the saving knowledge of Jesus Christ, disciple and equip them, and provide spiritual leadership while striving to maintain the Pentecostal landmarks of the Church.

name: International Business Student Organization
description: Baylor IBSO is an organization meant to provide all members the opportunity to build their network, learn about global business practices, and attend guest speeches from people with firsthand experience in the international business environment. With the world getting more and more interconnected students need to grow their understanding of what international business looks like which is Baylor IBSO’s main mission. All business major’s minor’s, and pre-business students are welcome and encouraged to join!
summary: ISBO provides Baylor business and pre-business students with dynamic opportunities to engage with business in an international context. The organization will equip its members with inherently valuable skills needed in today’s interconnected world.

name: Undergraduate Federalist Society at Baylor University
description: The Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy provides a forum for discussion and debate over legal topics and networking among peers with an interest in the law. It is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be. The Society seeks both to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application through its activities. On Baylor's undergraduate campus specifically, the chapter's purpose will be to introduce discussion and debate of legal issues to undergraduate students.
summary: The Undergraduate Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy provides a forum for discussion and debate over legal topics and networking among peers with an interest in the law.

name: Baylor Wrestling Club
summary: The Baylor Wrestling Club is a student organization dedicated to growing a community of fellow wrestlers to improve and sustain athletic and defense capabilities.

name: Green Outlook
description: Green Outlook is a dynamic and inclusive student organization dedicated to promoting holistic health, well-being, and sustainability. We bring together students from diverse academic backgrounds to explore the intersections of nutrition, exercise, mindfulness, and public policy, creating a supportive community where knowledge, research, and practical strategies come together to improve the preparedness of future professionals.
summary: Green Outlook is a dynamic and inclusive student organization dedicated to promoting holistic health,well-being, and sustainability.

name: Astro at Baylor
description: Astro at Baylor is a multidisciplinary student organization aiming to bring rocket engineering and space technology to Baylor students through hands-on projects, competitions, and industry involvement. Each year, we design, build, and test a fully flight-ready 12–20 ft tall high-powered rocket capable of reaching heights of 30,000 ft. We also have a dedicated liquid rocket engine development team, giving members experience in propulsion, design, manufacturing, and testing. Our goal is to prepare our members for success after graduation in any industry or discipline.
summary: This organization allows students to gain hands-on rocket engineering experience through project and competition teams.

name: Pre-Law Chapter of National Black Law Students Association
description: The Baylor University Pre-Law Chapter of the National Black Law Students Association (NBLSA) is dedicated to empowering Black students with aspirations in law and legal studies. Our mission is to cultivate a supportive and inclusive community that fosters academic excellence, professional development, and social justice advocacy. Through mentorship, networking, leadership opportunities, and service initiatives, we aim to prepare our members for successful legal careers while amplifying Black voices within the legal pipeline. We are committed to promoting unity, equity, and representation within both the Baylor community and the broader legal field.
summary: The Baylor Pre-Law Chapter of National Black Law Students Association is committed to creating a space for black students at Baylor University that are interested in law and legal studies, promoting professionalism, community service, and leadership.

name: Taiwanese American Student Association
description: The purpose of our organization is to bring together individuals from diverse backgrounds to celebrate and promote Asian and Taiwanese cultures on campus.
summary: The purpose of our organization is to bring together individuals from diverse backgrounds to celebrate and promote Asian and Taiwanese cultures on campus.

name: Makers at BU
description:
This organization exists to facilitate an environment for Baylor students to gather,
discuss and collaborate on various personal or group projects in the fields of
technology, engineering and digital or electronic art. The primary focus would be
to create an environment where Baylor students can gather to explore, create, and
engage with all aspects of making. This includes not individualized making but
also designing projects, improving makes with supportive feedback, group
crafting, and teaching one another.
summary: Makers at BU facilitates an environment for Baylor students to gather, discuss and collaborate on various personal or group projects in the fields of technology, engineering and digital or electronic art.

name: F1 Club
description:
The F1 Club at Baylor 

Are you a die-hard Formula 1 fan or just discovering the world of racing? Whether you're cheering for Ferrari, Mercedes, Red Bull, or just F1 in general, the F1 Club at Baylor is your home for all things motorsport. 

We’re more than just spectators; we're a community. The F1 Club brings together students from all backgrounds who share a passion for motorsports. From watch parties and fantasy league rivalries to real-life kart racing events, we fuel your love for motorsports with unforgettable experiences on and off campus. 

Whether you're in it for the social scene, the engineering, or the adrenaline rush, you'll find your people here. Our club is free, inclusive, and always open to new fans and seasoned pros alike.
summary: The F1 Club at Baylor is an organisation dedicated to bringing together students who are fans of Formula 1 from all backgrounds by hosting events that promote socialisation through kart racing and connecting a free and open community.

name: Fishing Club at Baylor
summary: Fishing Club at Baylor provides recreational fishing and socialization opportunities for all students that are interested. The goal is to provide a club where students who are looking for community can be embraced regardless of their fishing experience.

name: Gather Dance
description: Join us for a weekly 1 hour class where we learn a new choreographed dance together! We’ll begin with a quick 10-minute warm-up, spend 45 minutes diving into choreography, and finish with a 5-minute cool down. At the end of the year, there's a possibility of performing in a recital/showcase.
summary: Gather Dance offers a space for students who danced in high school and wish to continue in college. While the club is primarily geared toward experienced dancers, all students are welcome to join and enjoy dancing together!

name: Gift of Life/Baylor Chapter
description: Gift of Life Marrow Registry is dedicated to saving lives by facilitating blood stem cell and marrow transplants for patients. The registry recruits volunteer donors who are matched with patients battling leukemia, lymphoma, and other blood-related diseases. At Baylor University, through the Campus Ambassador Program, we work to expand this life-saving mission by raising awareness, educating students about the donation process, and hosting swab drives to recruit new potential donors. By organizing events, collaborating with student organizations, and engaging with the campus community, we aim to increase representation in the registry and give more patients a second chance at life.
summary: Gift of Life's purpose is to cure blood cancer and related diseases through cellular therapies by connecting patients in need with life-saving donors.

name: Financial Literacy Mentors at Baylor
description: The purpose of Financial Literacy Mentors at Baylor is to educate and mentor middle and high school students on essential financial principles by connecting them with Baylor University
summary: The purpose of Financial Literacy Mentors at Baylor is to educate and mentor middle and high school students on essential financial principles by connecting them with Baylor University students.

name: Athletic Students in Health
description: ASH is an organization with the goal of raising donations for and awareness of sports in the less fortunate Waco Community. We will provide sports equipment, nutrition, and trainings.
summary: Athletic Students in health works in the local community to promote youth sports.

name: Student Film Association
summary: The Student Film Association aims to oster a community of collaboration, creativity, character, philanthropic action, and lifelong bonds by and between students of Film.

name: Student Aquarist Society at Baylor
summary: SASB is a group for students with fish aquariums to freely ask questions and gain knowledge about keeping fish. This organization is for all levels of fish keepers and is open to anyone who is potentially interested in having an aquarium.

name: Healing Hands for Women and Children - Dallas Campus
summary: Healing Hands for Women and Children supports women and children who are victims of domestic violence by promoting women's health and well-being in both healthcare settings and beyond

name: Nigerian Student Association
description:
Nigerian Students Association of Baylor University (NSABU)

The Nigerian Students Association of Baylor University (NSABU) is a student-led organization dedicated to enhancing the academic, cultural, and social experiences of Nigerian students at Baylor University. The association serves as a supportive community for Nigerian undergraduate and graduate students, as well as Nigerian faculty and staff, fostering connection, belonging, and mutual support within the university environment.

NSABU exists to connect Nigerian students to academic, professional, and leadership development opportunities both on campus and beyond. A central focus of the association is supporting newly admitted and prospective Nigerian students as they transition into life at Baylor, helping them navigate academic expectations, campus resources, and cultural adjustment. Through mentorship, information sharing, and peer support, the association works to ensure that students feel welcomed and equipped to succeed.

In collaboration with Baylor University and the Center for Global Engagement, NSABU also promotes intercultural exchange by creating opportunities for Nigerian students to engage with other international and domestic students. Through educational programs, cultural events, and social activities, the association celebrates Nigerian culture while encouraging dialogue, understanding, and cross-cultural learning within the wider Baylor community.

The organization operates under a structured executive leadership team and holds regular meetings to plan programs, manage organizational affairs, and advance its mission. Membership is open to eligible Nigerian students, faculty, and staff who are committed to the values of community, responsibility, and academic excellence. Through collective leadership and active participation, NSABU aims to build a vibrant, engaged, and supportive Nigerian student community at Baylor University.
summary: The Nigerian Students Association of Baylor University exists to support and advance the social, cultural, academic, and professional needs and interests of prospective, incoming, current, and former Nigerian students of Baylor University.

name: Professional Writing and Rhetoric Student Organization
description: The PWR student organization exists to build community around writing at Baylor.
summary: The PWR Student Organization aims to build community among students majoring in Professional Writing and Rhetoric, and among other students who are interested in PWR.

name: Liberty in North Korea/Baylor Chapter
summary: Liberty in North Korea at Baylor (LiNK Baylor) is a student-led chapter of the international nonprofit Liberty in North Korea. Our mission is to raise awareness about the ongoing humanitarian crisis in North Korea.

name: Army Cadet Organization at Baylor
summary: The Army Cadet Organization's primary goals are fundraising for Army ROTC and promoting community events with veteran involvement.

name: Medical Humanities Community
description:
The Medical Humanities Program is made up of different types of students - Pre - majors, majors, minors, BIC specializations, DeBakey Scholars, MH student workers. The MH Community has the goal of making the program more than just a specialization - it is a place to build relationships, share stories, and turn ideas into meaningful projects.

Come share your story, meet others, and discover the events, projects, and opportunities waiting for you in the Medical Humanities Program.

 

Join our GroupMe groupchat to keep up to date with all the events: https://groupme.com/join_group/110758978/21OnYHfb
summary: The community of students, professors and directors of the Medical Humanities Program at Baylor University.

name: Healing Harmonies
description:
Our goal is to bring the gift of music and joy to our community here in Waco! We visit assisted living centers, hospitals, libraries, schools, etc. every other week to perform. Meetings are only once a month, and members can earn service hours by performing. Musicians of all skill levels are welcome to join our community! 
Please fill out the New Member Application when joining!
summary: Our goal is to bring the gift of music and joy to our community here in Waco! We will visit assisted living centers, hospitals, libraries, schools, etc. every other week to perform.

name: National American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative/Baylor Chapter
summary: The mission of ALCSI is to promote awareness of lung cancer screening for individuals at high risk of developing lung cancer at Baylor University and the larger local community.

name: Scientists Promoting Awareness, Research, and Knowledge
summary: The purpose of SPARK is to empower graduate students and postdoctoral scholars in science at Baylor University

name: 180 Degrees Consulting/Baylor Chapter
description:
180 Degrees Consulting at Baylor University is a student led chapter of the broader global organization, 180Degrees Consulting. 180Degrees Consulting's mission is to partner with nonprofits and social enterprises to deliver high quality, data driven consulting services at no cost. Our chapter's mission is to create meaningful social impact within our Waco community while equipping our members with professional skills that will aid them regardless of their chosen professional careers. 

 

If you're interested in social impact, and driving meaningful change for non-profits, we encourage you to apply! We are non-major specific, and in fact across other branches around the world many 180DC chapters are filled with students studying different fields.
summary: 180 Degrees Consulting at Baylor University provides pro bono consulting services to nonprofits and social enterprises while giving students real world consulting experience in strategy, analytics, and impact driven problem solving.

name: Minorities in Sports Business at Baylor University
description: The mission of Minorities in Sports Next at Baylor is to bridge the access gap between aspiring minority students and the professional sports business industry. As part of the global MIS network, we proactively empower our members by providing essential resources, facilitating strong connections with industry leaders, and securing access to elite career opportunities. Our goal is to foster a diverse and representative sports business landscape that reflects the communities and consumers driving the industry.
summary: Minorities in Sports Business provides Baylor students with the necessary resources, connections, and information that will allow them to grow and gain opportunities in the sports business industry.

name: PitStop
summary: Pitstop was created with the intention of cultivating a supportive and inclusive space where women can share their love for racing, build meaningful relationships, and create opportunities for exciting experiences.

name: Hearts for the Homeless
summary: The goal of our organization is to provide for, offer support to, and advocate on behalf of displaced individuals and those experiencing homelessness within the Waco community.

name: Waco Symphony Orchestra Student Ambassadors
description: The Waco Symphony Orchestra Student Ambassadors (WSOSA) connects Baylor University students with the Orchestra’s mission to enrich Central Texas’s cultural and artistic life. Through volunteering, advocacy, and outreach, WSOSA helps students become arts supporters and future nonprofit leaders. Unlike typical volunteer groups, WSOSA offers hands-on experience in arts administration, including governance, fundraising, and audience development. Students take part in board training, grant writing, budgeting exercises, and learn how nonprofits operate. Masterclasses and workshops with Baylor faculty and local arts professionals bridge academic learning and real-world arts impact. WSOSA focuses on inclusive fundraising and growing audiences through community research and data-driven outreach, linking more people to live classical music. While closely connected to the Waco Symphony Orchestra, WSOSA also partners independently with Baylor’s nonprofit leadership programs and student-led service groups. Despite symphony being in the name, the program is for all students in any discipline, as long as they like art! The WSOSA is personalized for your career goals and made to diversify and amplify your experience.
summary: WSO Student Ambassadors unite Baylor students with the Waco Symphony to advance local arts philanthropy, drive campus engagement, and harness personal and professional growth through nonprofit support and music advocacy.
